### Abraham — 2020-01-13T18:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-284517>

Hi all,

My first time posting on this forum, and I apologise for going over the top, but it is a long one…

If this thread is an innapropriate place to post this, or any individual part of this, please let me know what I should repost, and where.

**First some background:**

To be clear, I'm not seeking qualified medical opinions on treatment of any health condition. I'm just looking for personal opinions based on peoples own research, knowledge and experience.

Feel free to offer opinion based on extrapolation of scientific studies or concepts you are aware of, personal experience, or even just intuition. I’m open to all of those valuable sources of information.

I've been looking into buying a red / infrared LED panel recently. Leaning towards the mitogen because of the low flicker, emf and THD etc. I’d love to hear feedback from Josh on my queries, as no doubt he’s done a lot of research during development of his panels, and would be the foremost authority on what his panels are capable of, and best suited to. However, these questions aren’t all specific to the mitogen, and there is obviously a wealth of knowledge and experience amongst the members of this forum. So please do chime in, anybody who feels they have something worth contributing.

My primary motivations for buying a panel are for use aimed at the following goals, the first two being top priority:

1\. To optimise my wife’s fertility - Both her fallopian tubes were previously diagnosed as being blocked, and we have been through several cycles of ivf previously to no avail. Her age, and as a result possibly egg quality is also a challenge. So looking at using photobiomodulation as a supplementary practice alongside another attempt at ivf in the near future. My reproductive system seems to be working ok according to lab results etc

2\. To optimise my thyroid function - I have an enlarged thyroid that appears to be primarily a result of long term severe iodine deficiency. My bloodtests do not indicate hypo or hyper thyroidism, or thyroid auto immune diseases. I am addressing this through a combination of monitoring with ultrasound, functional medicine testing, and treating it through nutrition and supplementation, in consultation with a holistic GP and a naturopath. I'm also doing my best to make the most of sun exposure and minimise artificial light exposure, to optimise my circadian rythyms and mitochondrial health etc. (particularly important given I do shift work). So planning on using photobiomodulation alongside these other modalities.

3\. Sleep optimisation – Any improvements to our sleep and circadian rhythms in general would be welcomed, particularly for me given that I do shift work .

4\. Aesthetic optimisation - Mainly for my wife, to reduce the signs of aging (good excuse to get her on board…). Also hoping it might help slow down or even reduce my male pattern baldness.

5\. General health optimisation – etc etc

......................................................................................................................................................

**EFFECTIVE WAVELENGTHS FOR FEMALE FERTILITY:**

Regarding photobiomodulation for female fertility, the only references to evidence I've found so far are studies or treatments done using:

A) Lasers at 830nm-

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3944482/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3944482/)

&

B). Lasers at 808nm, combined with LEDs at 660nm-

[https://www.powermediclasers.com/en...y/160-laser-therapy-and-fertility-in-practice](https://www.powermediclasers.com/en/news/77-news-about-laser-therapy/160-laser-therapy-and-fertility-in-practice)

The majority consensus seems to be that LEDs of sufficent power at appropriate wavelengths have achieved results equivalent to lasers in all applications they've been tested in so far. So I assume LEDs and lasers are (in theory) likely to be similarly effective for female fertility applications.

Regarding the wavelength, most of the LED panels I've seen use a 50/50 combo of 850nm & 660nm. Exceptions being:

A) The platinum LED biomax, offering a small percentage of LEDs at 630nm, 810nm, 830nm, with the majority of the LEDs at 850nm & 660nm.

B) Redlight rising full stack, offering custom fill of

LEDs in - 610nm, 630nm, 660nm, 670nm, 740nm, 760nm, 830nm, 850nm (and other wavelengths by request).

**QUESTION- Can anybody shed some light… on whether the specific wavelengths of 850nm & 660nm common in LED panels (such as the mitogen) are likely to be similarly effective to the specific wavelengths of 830nm, 808nm used in the laser fertility treatment protocols?**

The panel will be used for my wife's fertility, my thyroid health, and general health for both of us, so I don't want to put too much focus on the fertility aspect, but if a small change in wavelengths of the unit we chose is likely to make a significant difference to it's effectiveness for optimising fertility, without sacrificing too much of it's effectiveness for other more general applications, then I guess now is the time to work that out, so we can purchase the best unit for our needs.

......................................................................................................................................................

**BODY COVERAGE & MINIMIZING TREATMENT SESSION TIMES:**

It seems that the mitogen, much like other similar sized “full body” panels are really sized for just doing head and torso in one application at the recommended treatment distance of 6-12 inches.

So if you only wanted to do just the torso, you could position it high, do the front, and then turn around to do the back (2 application periods/positions).

Or if you wanted to do head to toe, do the same as above, then lower the panel and do front and back again (4 application periods/positions).

Or better yet, if you had 2 panels stacked one on top of the other you could do the whole body head to toe by doing the front in one application, then turn around and do the back (2 application periods/positions).

Or do just the torso sandwiched between 2 panels, 1 front and 1 rear (1 application period/position).

Ideally you’d have 4 panels, so you could do front and back full length in 1 application period/position.

**QUESTION- Does the above sound accurate?**

......................................................................................................................................................

**ALTERNATIVE SIMILAR PRICED PANEL FOR FULL BODY LENGTH COVERAGE:**

The platinum biomax 900, and even the 600 has some pretty bold claims made that indicate around 100mw/cm2 at 18 inches out from the panel, and with 90 degree beam angle LEDs - the light spread at that distance creating a footprint that could easily cover me head to toe (Biomax-600= 72”x45” / Biomax-900= 72”x49”). However I’m sceptical about these claims. Also, I do wonder if being that far out (even if those numbers are accurate for overall power density at that distance), would result in less light penetrating enough to reach deep tissues. Which I assume would be important for the more direct and less systemic aspects of a fertility application. She could probably do close up, focussed, short duration applications on the abdomen. Then further distance, longer duration applications for full length of body and general health. However, if the whole point of buying a higher powered panel is to allow a full length treatment in one application to save time, this kind of defeats the purpose to a certain extent.

**QUESTION-1- Does the above sound accurate?**

**QUESTION-2- Aside from standing further away to increase the light footprint of a given panel for greater coverage purposes, is there really much benefit in altering treatment distances (and maybe turning on/off the red/infrared to isolate one or the other), to maximize effectiveness of particular treatment applications (eg skin vs deep tissues/organs). Or is it just more time, for not significantly different results than you would get using a consistant moderate treatment distance with both the red and infrared turned on each time to kill 2 birds with one stone?**

………………………………………………………………………………………………

**IMPORTANCE/VALUE OF FULL BODY LENGTH TREATMENTS (INCLUDING LEGS):**

**QUESTION- Do you think there is much value in doing the legs on a regular basis?**

Obviously if you had specific ailments in that area, like skin conditions or damage to deeper tissue, then it would be valuable to do some targeted applications to speed up the healing process. What I mean is, as part of an ongoing regimen of daily application for overall preventative maintenance and health/performance optimisation, whether doing the full length of your body would make a significant difference, or whether it would be a case of not much extra return for your investment of time (doing extra area application with the same panel), or money (buying an extra light and using it to do full length of body in one application).

…………………………………………………………………………………………………

**THE IMPORTANCE OF LOW LEVELS OF FLICKER IN A PANEL:**

I have read/heard some people claim flicker is not a big deal in this context, my feeling is that it probably is.

I guess if you look at it on face value from a reductionist perspective, you are only being exposed to it for 10 -20 minutes a day. So it might seem to be of little consequence compared to the likely far longer periods daily most of us will spend exposed to other sources of artificial light with high levels of flicker (even if you factor in proximity to, and intensity of the source). But my intuition tells me that it might be more important with photobiomodulation to emulate as closely as possible the way red and infrared are delivered by the sun (flicker free), so the body responds in the same manner. In other words, the subtle differences in the way the body receives and responds to the light when it's flickering may inhibit some of the beneficial processes, and or induce some other counterproductive effects. So the loss of benefit of 10-20 minutes in front of a high flicker panel vs a low flicker panel, ends up being far more significant to your overall health than the detriment of the same amount of time in front of another high flicker artificial light source which is not part of a health/healing treatment. Not exactly the same, but on one level a bit like how emfs etc have a more significant impact on the body during sleep/recovery than while awake and active. I’m sure this is a question with many multi layered answers, however…

**QUESTION- How important is flicker in a photobiomodulation panel, and why?**

…………………………………………………………………………………………………

**IMPACT OF INFRARED SAUNA ON MALE FERTILITY:**

I am currently doing an infrared sauna fornightly at a nearby float centre (mainly for detox reasons). I read somewhere that studies have shown regular saunas had a significant temporary impact on male fertility. Furthermore that 6 months was required for sperm markers to return to normal after sauna activity was ceased, however I think the participants were taking saunas much more frequently than fortnightly, and I think from memeory it was traditional steam sauna, not infrared.

**QUESTION- I would be interested to hear opinions on whether a fortnightly frequency of infrared sauna would be likely to have a significant impact on male fertility, and what the likely fertility recovery time would be after ceasing taking saunas at this frequency.**

…………………………………………………………………………………………………

Thanks,

Abraham

So the loss of benefit of 10-20 minutes in front of a high flicker panel vs a low flicker panel, ends up being far more significant to your overall health than the detriment of the same amount of time in front of another high flicker artificial light source which is not part of a health/healing treatment. Not exactly the same, but on one level a bit like how emfs etc have a more significant impact on the body during sleep/recovery than while awake and active. I’m sure this is a question with many multi layered answers, however…
>
>
>
> **QUESTION- How important is flicker in a photobiomodulation panel, and why?**
>
> …………………………………………………………………………………………………
>
>
>
>
> **IMPACT OF INFRARED SAUNA ON MALE FERTILITY:**
>
>
>
> I am currently doing an infrared sauna fornightly at a nearby float centre (mainly for detox reasons). I read somewhere that studies have shown regular saunas had a significant temporary impact on male fertility. Furthermore that 6 months was required for sperm markers to return to normal after sauna activity was ceased, however I think the participants were taking saunas much more frequently than fortnightly, and I think from memeory it was traditional steam sauna, not infrared.
>
>
>
> **QUESTION- I would be interested to hear opinions on whether a fortnightly frequency of infrared sauna would be likely to have a significant impact on male fertility, and what the likely fertility recovery time would be after ceasing taking saunas at this frequency.**
>
> …………………………………………………………………………………………………
>
>
> Thanks,
>
>
> Abraham

Your shift work will never be optimized by any panels...... Inconvenient to hear but true. Think of it like this.... You are a human. You are trying to live the life of a raccoon, mouse, or some other nocturnal animal. Your cells that are 70 trillion are optimized to Ultraviolet, blue, Red, Infrared, wavelengths of sunlight. Can you get out of shift work? Where do you live?

What is wife's age? Does she work nights? What is the health of her mother, grandmother? That will tell you about her engines she inherited.

Also NEVER underestimate the power of sunlight. It is the most powerful drug on the planet.

Regarding thyroid cold immersion can stimulate thyroid production. I like to think of it this way. The body is trying to protect itself. If you are under blue light at night your immune system constantly has to make cells. It is what it does under that light. The body lowers the temperature so that it can make those immune cells easier thus the lower thyroid. My body temperature was 97.5 for a decade. I do cold immersion and Sun and showers all cold. My core body temperature is 98.6-99.00. I never worked nights but I had chronic insomnia so sleeping was trashed for over 15 years. I am 44 now and after doing what is recommended her I sleep almost as good as I did when I was 20.

The basic building blocks start with fatty acids, specifically DHA; every human cell requires it. When it is sparse, we loss cognitive function as well. Proteins also require DHA for it phospholipid bilayer membrane; thus cold water “sea food”. Minerals are use with fatty acids and proteins to build every human cell; thus Dr. Jack Kruse recommends oysters. Thanks for the refresher, John. The more I read here - and on Jack's blogs - the more I learn about the necessary ingredients for being optimal. Since I discovered Jack Kruse in 2015, I've added substantially more seafood to my diet. (My favorite is ahi tuna and sockeye salmon steaks, or raw in poke salads). Oysters more than ever. Rarely a day goes by without something from the sea. One thing I haven't added is seaweed. I'll be doing that soon. Also added lots of sunshine, starting a few minutes first thing in the morning, often an hour or so in the middle of the day, and ending up in late afternoon sunshine on the back patio for 30-60 minutes. CT so far only in winter, although I'm looking into what I can to to get that chiller I mentioned on the CT chamber experiment thread.

The light environment is the foundation of optimal, which is why this thread about red light therapy caught my attention. I've learned a bit from pain specialists (mostly naturopaths) who have phenomenal success with lasers of different colors. Josh's products go deeper, actually into tissue repair. At the moment, I'm most interested in red light therapy for skin health. I already have a lot of the ingredients for good collagen, etc.. I'm sure I could do more in this regard.

All that said, I've come to grasp the concept of _**context**_. Adding ingredients to my health recipe is only part of the answer. The context of how my body uses them determines where they go and what they do when they get there. (Case in point: taking calcium supplements for bone health is irrelevant - and even harmful - when calcium metabolism isn't working well. Lots to that story, as I'm sure you already know.) As we both know, living a blue-lit, circadian mismatch lifestyle will undermine everything else we do.

My context is that my body is not assimilating electrons and light information as well as it could. I'm with you about a previous comment you've made regarding how we use waveforms. Quantum biology is a whole new ballgame for me. The more I understand about it, the better I can deduce what my most effective biohacks can be. Thus, long-windedly, my intrest in red light/photobiomodulation therapy.

Hi, I have both panels and Mitogen is too weak. This EMR panel is much better: [https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm](https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm) I think I would have bought 2-6 Firestorm and put these together into one larger panel.

### Sajid Mahmood — 2022-01-31T15:14:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309368>

> [Johan Lindstrøm said:](/goto/post?id=309367)
>
> Hi, I have both panels and Mitogen is too weak. This EMR panel is much better: [https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm](https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm) I think I would have bought 2-6 Firestorm and put these together into one larger panel.

I think 2 firestorms is best, using 3 or more firestorms together may cause circuit problems. Also with more power, it’s better to use in shorter durations, because longer durations leads to biphasic dose response and tissue damage. You have to figure out what the right duration, and distance should be. The distance has to be good enough that it penetrates through.

### JanSz — 2022-01-31T16:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309369>

1060watts/115 volts=9.2 amps

On 15 amps circuits, you can have only one of these lamps
On 20 amps circuits, you can have (barely) two

15A circuits (usually) are on 14 ga wires (if you are lucky, they may be 12 ga) (then you may replace them with 20A outlets (and 20 A breaker). Worth checking.
20A circuits are on 12 ga wires.

Sometimes it is easy to add a new electric panel.
It was not easy, but I had to do it for my DW's saltwater aquarium. Did wires good for 100A (planning) but put it on 50A breaker.
Then it was easy to get numerous 15 or 20A clustered outlets in just the right places.

### Johan Lindøm — 2022-01-31T20:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309374>

You do not want to sit too close to these panels, they emit dirty current. If I have about 10 cm distance from Firestrom for 10 minutes, this is sufficient. Normally I never use these panels for more than 20 minutes at a time. After 20 minutes, some biological effect occurs that we do not want, do not remember which one. Normally I have a distance of one to three feet between me and the light source often I use UVB with these panels.

### John Schumacher — 2022-01-31T20:37:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309375>

Also with more power, it’s better to use in shorter durations, because longer durations leads to biphasic dose response and tissue damage. You have to figure out what the right duration, and distance should be. The distance has to be good enough that it penetrates through.

[@Sajid Mahmood](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19802/) - Please note the human studies are researching the "sweet spot" for "biphasic dose response"; these are not "currently" a function of time, but a function of J/cm2.

Specifically, what has been found "so far" is J/cm2 in range between 50 and 100 J/cm2 is the biphasic dose/response curve producing deleterious effects.
At doses with lower values (30 and 50 J/cm2 ), it was possible to observe positive effects such as stimulation and cell proliferation.

However, most studies are conducted using "professional low level laser" machines. I believe these could be generalized to other products like the EMR Firestorm. To do so, we must understand the mechanism of action, depth of penetration, focus of the light (how narrow the beam). Then we must test using biomarkers which indicate efficacy of "how well" each perform.

Source:

Biphasic Dose/Response of Photobiomodulation Therapy on Culture of Human Fibroblasts
[https://sci-hub.se/10.1089/photob.2019.4729](https://sci-hub.se/10.1089/photob.2019.4729)​

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309432>

How to calculate:

500 W/m^2 = 0.05 J/cm^2
70-75 mWatts/cm^2 = 70-75 J/cm^2​

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) reported on post -> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/red-light-panels.26557/page-3#post-308297](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/red-light-panels.26557/page-3#post-308297)

The EMR Firestorm produced -> at 15-16" 70-75 mWatts/cm^2 , (every 15 seconds you get a one Joule of energy)
10 - 60 Joule for deep tissue treatment---> 2.5 minutes to 15 minutes, 15"-16" away
​

However, please note when comparing types of photobiomodulation machines -> low level lasers are _very narrow beamed_ devices. Most LED devices "we" can purchase have a beam spectrum as wide as 180 degrees. Please consider reading:

The Nuts and Bolts of Low-level Laser (Light) Therapy
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3288797/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3288797/)​

-   Spatial coherence typically is expressed through the output being a narrow beam which is diffraction-limited, often a so-called “pencil beam.”​

-   These narrow beams can cut a hole in your skin.​

​

This is why I stated above: Most studies are conducted using "professional low level laser" machines. I believe these could be generalized to other products like the EMR Firestorm. To do so, we must understand the mechanism of action, depth of penetration, focus of the light (how narrow the beam). Then we must test using biomarkers which indicate efficacy of "how well" each perform.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2022-02-03T05:16:04+0200

>

The the problem of J/cm2 used as dose....
Lets say that a medium/ large potted plant needs a gallon of water a week. A Gallon applied by a pressure washer in 10 seconds would dig the plant out of the pot. That would be surgical use of a water just like a surgical laser. if it was poured in over a 2 mins then it would drown the plant in the short term but within a week the water would be dispersed / soaked up / evaporated and at the end of a week the plant might have recovered. Just don't do that every week to the poor plant, maybe better to drip it in slowly over a one week period then no harm done. You are putting it in as fast as the plant is using it. The light bed MK1 was low density light, about 17mW/cm2 and I'm not saying you can stay in it all day, even 17mW/cm2 for over an hour (61J/cm2) might be too much, we don't know the limit yet for humans, but is not the same as 61J/cm2 applied in 30 seconds. that would be analgesic (neural blockade). Having said that, nice interesting things seem to happen with high dose low intensity light . some people feel euphoric, many fall asleep (including me, every time).

There are studies that show 30Joules of energy delivered with 5mw/cm2 over a longer time was more effective than when delivered by 50mw/cm2 in a shorter time, for example.

We get these questions all the time and my general response is that westerners DO NOT UNDERSTAND SUBLETY, AND ALWAYS THINK MORE IS BETTER (hint, it is not)

joules/dose

/time chart from mk1.6 mitogen pod

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2022-02-03T05:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309502>

What type of Watts is important, and how many Watts do we need?

The intensity, sometimes called the Power Density, Irradiance, and units of mW/cm^2 tends to be very important. This is what is most often reported in the clinical science when talking about dose. Typically the effective range is between 5mW/cm^2 to 50mW/cm^2 in most panels.

Of course, more intensity does not always mean better results, and sometimes using lower power lights for [longer time](https://gembared.com/blogs/musings/how-long-should-you-use-red-light-therapy-for-another-dimension-to-dosing) is more practical. Lower intensities might be used in products that are used with [skin contact](https://gembared.com/blogs/musings/proper-red-light-therapy-dosing-contact-method-vs-non-contact-method), where higher intensities are used for panels that are [further away](https://gembared.com/blogs/musings/what-distance-should-we-be-from-our-red-light-panel-a-history-lesson) to counteract the reflection losses. Eventually, much intensity will start to cause heating or eye concerns.

### John Schumacher — 2022-02-03T20:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309519>

> [Sajid Mahmood said:](/goto/post?id=309518)
>
> The target is to get just the right amount of irradiance to get through to the deeper tissues with the least amount of bipashic dose response.
>
> What irradiance is just enough to penetrate through to the deeper tissues, also with the least amount of bipasic dose response.

Penetration is a function of wavelength, coherence, time, and the tissue type.
There are no formulas for calculating these depths. However, we do know:

Penetration generally increases as wavelength values get larger.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4552256/#b99-ndt-11-2191](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4552256/#b99-ndt-11-2191)

​

Coherence of the waveform increases penetration as its beam form narrows into a focal point.

The Importance of Coherency

[https://sci-hub.se/10.1089/pho.2005.23.431](https://sci-hub.se/10.1089/pho.2005.23.431)​

All the photons emitted vibrate in phase agreement both in space and time.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2825126/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2825126/)​

Tissue type - recent tissue studies demonstrate no penetration of low level NIR energy through 2 cm of skull and brain.

Near-infrared photonic energy penetration: can infrared phototherapy effectively reach the human brain?

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4552256/#b99-ndt-11-2191](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4552256/#b99-ndt-11-2191)​

Irradiance (mW/cm2) is the intensity and power density of the emitted light over time.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6968738/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6968738/) ​

​

### Sajid Mahmood — 2022-02-06T21:52:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309615>

Is an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² enough to penetrate through to the torso, or doesn’t the irradiance really matter for penetration? Do you just need a longer duration with that sort of irradiance?

From all the information on here, it looks as if an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² will give the least amount of biphasic dose response, but obviously a longer dose is required.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2022-02-11T22:49:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309954>

> [Sajid Mahmood said:](/goto/post?id=309615)
>
> Is an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² enough to penetrate through to the torso, or doesn’t the irradiance really matter for penetration? Do you just need a longer duration with that sort of irradiance?
>
> From all the information on here, it looks as if an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² will give the least amount of biphasic dose response, but obviously a longer dose is required.

depth is determined by wavelength primarily. even near infrared, whilst technically penetrating up to 2-3cm, loses some intensity as it gets to the deeper tissues. say 10% gets to the deeper stuff, that would be 1mw/cm2 if using 10mw.

clinically i see better results with lower intensities say 15 to 30mw/cm2 for longer periods being the best and safest.

### John Schumacher — 2022-02-13T17:53:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-309998>

> [Sajid Mahmood said:](/goto/post?id=309615)
>
> Is an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² enough to penetrate through to the torso, or doesn’t the irradiance really matter for penetration? Do you just need a longer duration with that sort of irradiance?
>
> From all the information on here, it looks as if an irradiance of 5 mW/m² to 10 mW/m² will give the least amount of biphasic dose response, but obviously a longer dose is required.

Light penetration is not a function of mW/m² - it is the coherency of the waveform over time into the density of the tissue type.

[http://photobiology.info/Coherence.html](http://photobiology.info/Coherence.html)

_Coherence volume_ (V**coh**) is coherence length (ΔL**coh**) multiplied to coherence area.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

For noncoherent light (LED and spectrally filtered light from a lamp).

When a tissue is irradiated by and LED L**coh**, length of temporal (longitudinal) coherence;

**coh**, size of spatial (lateral) coherence; D, diameter of light beam; d, diameter of noncoherent-light source; ϕ, beam divergence,

, beam spectral width.
​

_Please note the design of the EMR-TEK Firestorm & Firehawk have focal lenses (what they call COBs). These focus the beam from a 120 angular spread to 60 degrees. Thus raising the potential of penteration by doubling the coherency of the waveform._

Surface layer of tissue (Δ

**surface**) and bulk tissue (a layer of a tissue in range of mm to cm thickness, depending on the type of tissue).​

I would likely say this depends on many things.
Intracellular water
Deuterium content
Photoreceptors/semiconductors
Melanin
Age / heteroplasmy
Hair?
Different tissues - fat/bone?

We know that with age, the synthesis of vitamin D is reduced. Would the same be true for red light / NIR? My guess is likely.

I think there are going to be a lot of variables, but I agree more is not always better.
Some people likely get too much stimulation for red light - whether that's the flicker, intensity, duration, their health.....

Light is medicine.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-07T03:24:56+0300

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-07T04:37:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-336780>

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-07T17:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-kruse-longevity-red-light-panels-pbm-from-josh.23224/post-336796>

Distance is a major factor. If you get close enough to an artificial light source, the apparent brightness (intensity you experience) can match that of the apparent brightness of sunlight on a bright day.

I don't think goggles are necessary in most cases either, but just something to consider

### Corey Nelson — 2019-03-01T18:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ddcenters-product.23225/post-264250>

It's a package that includes (some unspecified quantity of) DDW meant to last 24 days, plus low-deuterium exogenous ketones, NAD supplement, and "a nootropic." Interesting idea, would be nice to see some quantified results with such a regimen (including longitudinally after the DDW ran out), even though it's more of a "quick fix." Obviously targeting people who have sticker shock at the usual price of a DDW regimen.

Even if I wanted to, I personally wouldn't buy it without more transparency on what you're getting (like the amount of DDW, for starters!), but maybe that's just me.

[https://www.ddcenters.com/product/mitochondria-cleanse/](https://www.ddcenters.com/product/mitochondria-cleanse/)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-01T18:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ddcenters-product.23225/post-264251>

Based upon what they have said publically I think this goes against what they have said about drinking water......if you're anti water drinking as Boros and Q said recently why would you take NAD+ given Jason Rabinowitz latest paper on NAD+?

MARKETING is the only reason I can think of.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-01T18:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ddcenters-product.23225/post-264252>

The oxidized form of NAD, NAD+, is an essential cofactor in various cellular biochemical reactions. Levels of NAD+ and its reduced form NADH vary with the availability of dietary energy and nutrients. Nicotinamide phosphoribosyltransferase (Nampt) generates a key NAD+ intermediate, nicotinamide mononucleotide, from nicotinamide and 5-phosphoribosyl 1-pyrophosphate. The liver, an important organ of NAD metabolism, is thought to release metabolic products such as nicotinamide and may control NAD metabolism in other organs. Moreover, NAD is an important regulator of the circadian rhythm. Liver-specific Nampt-deficient mice and heterozygous Nampt mice have abnormal daily plasma Pi concentration oscillations. These data indicate that NAD metabolism in the intestine, liver, and kidney is closely related to Pi metabolism through the SLC34 family.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-01T18:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ddcenters-product.23225/post-264255>

Here is the NAD+ paper from 2018: [https://www.cell.com/cell-metabolism/pdfExtended/S1550-4131(18)30196-7](https://www.cell.com/cell-metabolism/pdfExtended/S1550-4131\(18\)30196-7)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-01T18:45:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ddcenters-product.23225/post-264256>

NAD+ infusions are fraught with major issues.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1550413116304259](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1550413116304259)

### jnash — 2019-03-01T19:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bells-palsy-help.23226/post-264268>

Hi, have a work colleague who had a case of 100% half-face paralysis Bell's Palsy a few months ago, under a lot of workload stress at the time. It eventually calmed down, but he still has issues today with left eye nerves etc. and getting worse again. Now, when he moves his jaw, it moves his left eye/eyelid. Acupuncture did not resolve at the time, needle in affected area was extremely painful, and advised to go back to his neurologist. I searched the Quilt and didn't find anything. Has had all 4 wisdom teeth removed ~15 years ago, no root canals. Male aged 34, 215 pounds, otherwise in good health.
Has been tested negative for Lyme. MRI scheduled a week away.
Any advice appreciated.
Thanks,
\-Jim

# Thread: Is drinking large qualities of water still recommended in light of deuterium considerations?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2019-03-02T04:07:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/post-264291>

I know Jack has historically recommended drinking large amounts of water.

Deuterium researchers recommend drinking only enough to not be thirsty. They do this because they argue that drinking more water will add more deuterium.

Given the consideration of the added deuterium, is it advised to drink to thirst, or drink a large quantity( say 4 litres) of water per day?

### Lahelada — 2019-03-02T12:50:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/post-264296>

Have you read this ? [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...egin-their-water-education.22759/#post-263470](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/where-do-physicians-begin-their-water-education.22759/#post-263470)
It does not tell you about how much you need as there is not a one size fits all answer but also tells you when you need to drink. What caps water intake in humans with good circadian clock control?

### Corey Nelson — 2019-03-02T16:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/post-264298>

> [mbhemming said:](/goto/post?id=264291)
>
> I know Jack has historically recommended drinking large amounts of water.
>
> Deuterium researchers recommend drinking only enough to not be thirsty. They do this because they argue that drinking more water will add more deuterium.
>
> Given the consideration of the added deuterium, is it advised to drink to thirst, or drink a large quantity( say 4 litres) of water per day?

The interesting thing is we don't really know how the statistics of deuterium depletion and deuterium homeostasis work across a large population or how sub-populations with different lifestyles and health parameters respond. Someone who operates a "center" will have access to methods and some limited data to attempt to answer this but that's far different from an actual controlled trial. I personally bash a lot of large scale studies because they're also biased and use bad analysis methods but there is a place in our epistemology for crunching numbers to answer certain questions (and humility when we haven't).

There is sound logic to water restriction and dry fasting for sure, and no doubt it is often effective, but I can think of many experimental designs that would answer questions we don't yet know the answer to, for example, when first starting a quantum health lifestyle could there be some benefits over time (even for deuterium homeostasis) to drinking greater quantities of water even if it's not DDW.

### Nardia — 2019-03-24T10:18:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/post-266324>

I have been drinking DDW for 6 weeks now and have noticed I am not as thirsty. I would normally have to drink 3 litres of RO water a day or I would get signs of a UTI and painful kidneys. The other day I only drank 1 litres of DDW all day and didn't need to pee for 12 hours and felt great without any thirst - I have never in my life been able to do this. I believe DDW has the capability to hydrate the cells more efficiently which may account for my lack of thirst and need to pee. DDW has given my the ability to have an even flow of energy all day without the extreme fatigue and roller-coaster energy flow I have been living with prior. My n=1 of cause!

### Sue-UK — 2019-03-25T11:25:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-drinking-large-qualities-of-water-still-recommended-in-light-of-deuterium-considerations.23227/post-266384>

[https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlehtml/2013/em/c3em00374d](https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlehtml/2013/em/c3em00374d) Human exposure to aluminium

As urine is an excretion route for aluminium, I think this puts a different slant on water intake.

Also if a lot of pork or pork products are eaten, and the animal has not been fed organically, chances are that the cereal based feed - and therefore the fat - is contaminated with glyphosate. Glyphosate is used to clear the ground of weeds, but also sprayed as a _**dessicant**_ prior to harvesting the crop.

[https://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/2019/Seneff\_February\_2019\_Hawaii.pdf](https://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/2019/Seneff_February_2019_Hawaii.pdf).

[https://www3.nd.edu/~aseriann/Glyphosate\_2.pdf](https://www3.nd.edu/~aseriann/Glyphosate_2.pdf) I need to print this out and go through it properly but from a metabolic water angle what drew my attention is the references to lipase.

If I have a high body burden of aluminium, and/or I am eating lipase disrupting glyphosate laced fat to make metabolic water ....

### Sue-UK — 2019-03-27T13:11:56+0200

From [file:///C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/1409759688Michelle-Sprent-report-2013%20(4).pdf](https://forum.jackkruse.com/file:///C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/1409759688Michelle-Sprent-report-2013%20\(4\).pdf) Sustainable pig nutrition

"Typically, UK pig feed formulations include wheat, barley, wheat feed, biscuit meal, extracted soya bean meal (hipro), rapeseed meal, sunflower meal and synthetic amino acids. In addition, I estimate that about 15-20% of UK finishing pigs are fed some form of liquid co-products for example brewer’s yeast, liquid whey, wheat distillers syrup."

I haven't eaten pork since December, as a hack, (and I feel better for it, so I suspect my redox can't handle it). Looking at the feed under the lens of glyphosate, think it might be off my menu for a while.  Interesting that Stephanie Seneff has said that sauerkraut and ACV contain acetobacter, one of the very few microbes that can metabolise glyphosate. Yogurt and kimchi probably do too (but that is a probably so I've chosen ACV as an add on to the hack).

>
> "Typically, UK pig feed formulations include wheat, barley, wheat feed, biscuit meal, extracted soya bean meal (hipro), rapeseed meal, sunflower meal and synthetic amino acids. In addition, I estimate that about 15-20% of UK finishing pigs are fed some form of liquid co-products for example brewer’s yeast, liquid whey, wheat distillers syrup."
>
> I haven't eaten pork since December, as a hack, (and I feel better for it, so I suspect my redox can't handle it). Looking at the feed under the lens of glyphosate, think it might be off my menu for a while.  **Interesting that Stephanie Seneff has said that sauerkraut and ACV contain acetobacter, one of the very few microbes that can metabolise glyphosate.** Yogurt and kimchi probably do too (but that is a probably so I've chosen ACV as an add on to the hack).

Another interesting thing Seneff has talked about is glyphosate taking aluminium into the pineal - hence my original hack of silicon rich water (a la Chris Exley's aluminium research) is now together with a bit of ACV as per Stephanie Seneff. If she's right, then I think that aluminium and/or glyphospate could negatively influence pineal function as badly as fluoride can, and if glyphosphate disrupts lipase that could affect ability to burn fat .... and if it gets into our proteins then I don't see why that and the pineal reference wouldn't affect how our bodies handle even natural light frequencies, and what could it be doing to _perception o_f circadian rhythms.......... So my question is whether glyphosate or aluminium can cause us to accumulate deuterium, either directly or by interfering with the deuterium depleting pathways .... ? # Thread: I'm not fucking crazy, but I am home
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-not-fucking-crazy-but-i-am-home.23228/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Akeemy McDreamy — 2019-03-02T14:01:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-not-fucking-crazy-but-i-am-home.23228/post-264297>

I am crazy when I was running barefoot in the summer and told people I felt better and my calluses were healing faster on my feet.
I am crazy because I wear speedos when I swim, sometimes I leave the house without a shirt and shoes becuase I rarely wear them becuase I love feeling the Texas sun on my skin..
I am crazy because I don't have any jackets and I pretty much wear shorts and t-shirts YEAR round . I've always thought that the human body should have the ability to adapt.
I am crazy becuase I don't like a lot of lights on when I read and prefer to go outside.
I am crazy becuase when I wake up in the morning I don't turn on the lights I either go outside and ground, sun bathe, do my wim hof and cold shower
I could go on and on, but before I found Dr. Jack Kruse inside I was alone. A couple years ago I was curious as to why races near the equator were more rhythmic than the white people from the Northern cold climates. I was researching how your absorption of sunlight varies by melanin if somehow melanin absorbed the rhythm of the sun. Was I actually crazy then? Probably. What really pissed me off was no one else was questioning it. They'd much rather not challenge anything, so after being the "crazy hippy" I started to become silent. I let the low dopamine energy vampires around me steal my soul.

Guess what! I got it back. I used everything I've learned from Dr. Jack Kruse and with about 10 days of. Training preparation I ran an Ultra Marathon last Sunday and I was in the gym squatting 405lbs with no belt. Everyone else has been paying attention to the wrong energy systems. I've started to train my body to improve my redox potential. I haven't taken the test outlined in the Epi Paleo prescription yet, but I've rewatched Nourish vermont 16-18 close to 30 times and who knows how many podcasts I've listened too. Anyways all that being said I no longer feel crazy, I feel at home. If any else thinks I'm crazy now. GOOD.

I'm very excited for this time in the world. The rift between the questioners vs the low dopamine followers is growing wider and wider. A HUGE SHIFT IS COMING people are waking up. I'm excited to be part of this group

Do we have any ultra endurance athletes, Calisthenics athletes, dancers, any movers and groovers using Black Swan Mitochondriac knowledge for performance?

“Everyone must leave something behind when he dies . . . Something your hand touched some way so your soul has somewhere to go when you die . . . It doesn't matter what you do, so long as you change something from the way it was before you touched it into something that's like you after you take your hands away.”

# Thread: 1 theory I have for why 5G is being rolled out
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-theory-i-have-for-why-5g-is-being-rolled-out.23230/>

### thrive1992 — 2019-03-03T05:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-theory-i-have-for-why-5g-is-being-rolled-out.23230/post-264335>

Theory :
Our planet is currently experiencing global cooling (thats why the media stopped calling it global warming and now say "climate change" - a term so vague that whatever happens they can say "told ya!")
With the grand solar minimum / global cooling there will be crop failures and food shortages.
The world ALREADY had many problems with supply and demand of food, due to poor government policies, people being disconnected with their food/farmer, environmental degradation, depletion of top soil ETC.
These problems will be made worse with coming climate change - weather extremes, increased cloud nucleation = less sun, generally colder.
In some countries like New Zealand, Australia, France that have very strong food production: It might not be so extreme.
But in countries/states/cities/regions that rely heavily on importing food like Japan, Singapore Korea etc, things could get chaotic when people freak out about the lack of food / food cost increases.
Population number/density, geographical isolation, culture, government etc these factors will also influence how things play out.
My theory is that governments are going for 5g not only because telco wants to make $$$$, but also because 5g is a convenient way to
\-crowd control
\-prevent chaos by ruining everyones health
\-potentially even for depopulation (decreasing the next generation population via causing infertility)

Let me know your thoughts.

If you want to learn about grand solar minimum and global cooling and food shortages check out youtube channels

People are talking on this forum about GSM and its implications, even Uncle Jack. I try to put in my 2 cents worth about this incipient crisis as appropriate.

That's an interesting theory you have there about implementing 5G to suppress the population, and even _cull the population_.

Actually, to me those potential evil effects are just unintended side effects of their wireless proliferation agenda.

I would not give **them** (entities working in an unorganized and incoherent manner to blanket earth with RF/MW radiation: telcos, gubmints, militaries, spooks, NGOs, academia, financial elites, black budget programs, and so on) any credit for attempting civilization's suicide by essentially weaponizing a technology like microwaves and RF.

**They** are just very clever but short-sighted idiots who happen to be running the show (or so they think in their conceit).

Your premise unfortunately falters when you consider that **they** and their loved ones are going to be destroyed by same wireless technology as effectively as will the unwashed masses. You don't really expect them to hunker down in EMF-safe bunkers for decades and chortle with glee as society disintegrates, now do you?

No, they ceaselessly move about planet on private jets and luxurious cars to run keep things running, and they love to take vacations around the world to show off their wealth and power.

Final endgame of wireless Armageddon is unclear to me, but I don't necessarily see it as an extinction event.

Based on info I have been gleaning about **earth catastrophe cycles** (yes they do exist as proven in scientific records) I am more worried about extinction level events that seem to be in our not-too-distant future, including a solar micro nova and magnetic pole reversal.

Modern-type humans have been around at least 250,000 years and have survived every earth cataclysm nature has thrown at them (although it is believed at one time only about 2000 humans worldwide survived a severe event).

We are a resilient species so I don't worry too much about our future. But civilizations would have to start all over again from a neolithic stage if it was bad enough. So what? Cosmic drama goes on forever.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-16T17:58:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-theory-i-have-for-why-5g-is-being-rolled-out.23230/post-265643>

> [thrive1992 said:](/goto/post?id=265619)
>
> "Your premise unfortunately falters when you consider that **they** and their loved ones are going to be destroyed by same wireless technology as effectively as will the unwashed masses. You don't really expect them to hunker down in EMF-safe bunkers for decades and chortle with glee as society disintegrates, now do you?" - Maybe the negative health affects of 5g etc won't take decades to occur? Also, there will be countries/locations that don't get 5g where they and their family can live a normal life. I disagree that they are "short sighted idiots" - the people at the top are organized and have been for a long time.
>
> "I am more worried about extinction level events that seem to be in our not-too-distant future, including a solar micro nova and magnetic pole reversal." - I am more interested in preparing for things that can be prepared for (How can one prepare for a magnetic pole reversal????????)
> What are your thoughts specifically on the Grand Solar Minimum ? Learning to grow, store food, manage animals and potentially moving to a low population density area are my focus.

Earth catastrophes are not necessarily **unsurvivable**. It all depends.

There is plenty of fearmongering and doom porn out there these days. Don't let it get you down. Whatever happens will happen-- the best revenge is to live happy lives in meantime and let fate keep its own counsel.

A major solar super flare or mini nova is certain to destroy all of our electric infrastructure (it is possible earth side pointing away from sun might fare better-- now one would know until it happens). If severe enough, the mass ejection from sun could literally fry sun-facing earth side and start massive conflagrations.

To be _Captain Obvious_ here--- our modern technologically advanced civilization runs on electricity and electronics and is very vulnerable. A solar event like this would throw us collectively back to medieval times or earlier.

There is a lot of conjecture about what a magnetic pole reversal would do. What we do know is that it happens on a time scale that reaches far back in geological history and earth upheavals are often the result.

The most alarming reversal theory is that when our geomagnetic field drops to zero during a reversal (it has happened in past) earth's rotation will slow to a standstill and then begin rotating in opposite direction. When our planet slows and halts, earth's atmosphere and oceans will keep on going in direction of rotation through inertia. Result: unimaginable winds of up to 1000 miles an hour (faster at equator than high latitudes) and tidal waves several miles high washing over entire continents.

It is believed humanity has cycled through many advanced civilizations over vast expanse of time and recurrent earth cataclysms that wiped them out. (And we today think we are pinnacle of human achievement!) No, no, no-- nature is about **cycles** and not linear progression.

But modern type humans (with our intellect and attributes) always managed to survive over the hundreds of thousands of years. Think about that feat.

Even SHTF worst case scenarios might be survivable to those sheltering underground (it is thought earlier humans sought shelter in caves and underground areas to survive these events).

One theory is that a sizeable portion of so-called missing $21 trillion missing from US budget went towards building unimaginably extensive underground facilities complete with supplies and environmental systems to survive such an event. Of course, there would be no accommodations there for ordinary folk.

Sun is likely to give off signs before he blows-- changes in sun color among others. That might give us early enough warning to 'duck and cover'.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-17T00:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-theory-i-have-for-why-5g-is-being-rolled-out.23230/post-265669>

> [thrive1992 said:](/goto/post?id=265619)
>
> "Your premise unfortunately falters when you consider that **they** and their loved ones are going to be destroyed by same wireless technology as effectively as will the unwashed masses. You don't really expect them to hunker down in EMF-safe bunkers for decades and chortle with glee as society disintegrates, now do you?" - Maybe the negative health affects of 5g etc won't take decades to occur? Also, there will be countries/locations that don't get 5g where they and their family can live a normal life. I disagree that they are "short sighted idiots" - the people at the top are organized and have been for a long time.
>
> "I am more worried about extinction level events that seem to be in our not-too-distant future, including a solar micro nova and magnetic pole reversal." - I am more interested in preparing for things that can be prepared for (How can one prepare for a magnetic pole reversal????????)
> What are your thoughts specifically on the Grand Solar Minimum ? Learning to grow, store food, manage animals and potentially moving to a low population density area are my focus.

As far as preparations for GSM go, it sounds like you are all over it like a donkey on a waffle. Keep going forward.

Learning how to grow food and obtain potable water (filtration and purification) are the keys. Even if you don't have a garden or yard you can grow indoors, even in an apartment. Be sure to use heirloom seeds-- other commercial variety seeds may or may not produce a seeding plant that will work when you save seeds for next year's crop (you may not be able to buy more seeds in a SHTF situation).

I would grow weak on a limited diet of just sprouts, fruits and green veggies. Therefore, I am studying grain and seed crops (quinoa, amaranth, millet, sunflower) and tubers (e.g potatoes) for starch and protein. I don't see this aspect of growing your own food talked about much.

Since solar minimums are associated with violent and erratic weather (which means risk of destructive hail storms increases), see if you can buy a small green house with shatter resistant panes to protect sensitive crops.

Depending on your USDA growing zone (check their _Plant Hardiness Zone Map_\-- in USA) it might be prudent to select plant varieties that are hardy up to 2 zones north of your area to preempt the probability that a developing GSM will shorten your traditional growing season and effectively shorten and make colder springs and falls.

In general, I'd say all usual advice for preppers would be applicable.

If you live in northern latitudes you could move to a more southern location to ride out the worst cold (unfortunately Canada might be mostly a frozen wasteland most of the year in worst case scenario.)

In any case, unless you are in a subtropical or tropical area it would make sense to add many more R values to your existing house insulation so that heating costs don't drive you into poverty. Supplemental/back up heating is a must so you don't freeze into a Popsicle when grid goes down in mid-winter. Wood and pellet stoves and propane heaters are good as long as they don't rely on household electricity to operate or start.

Without knowing which scenario might be more devastating I agree you Drezy Gaining skills and remaining flexible.

My goal is to optimize my mental and physical health, improve community, and develop a wide skill set.
When something happens this will allow to respond quickly and rationally.

It's not always the disaster that gets us killed, it is our response to it.
Paradise, CA is a good example. Some people made a choice to leave and others thought they would ride it out. Even then, the people that left late would have been okay but they chose the wrong way to exit the city.

I can prepare for a terrorist attack, a pole shift, and WW Three, fall prey to govt. mind control or have a health crisis and my preparations are pointless..

Develop community,(friends you can trust and friends who will speak truth). if people don't want to question their own beliefs, narratives and go beyond superficial interactions, they may as well go back in the house and watch some more TV.

I'm new here, is this oversharing?

### Lahelada — 2019-03-13T17:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wake-up-at-2am.23232/post-265351>

I think you have a mixed set of knowledge. It is never a good idea to play Mambo Nr 5 with information from so called experts without evaluating their clinical experience and for that you need knowledge yourself. You need to learn about light, stability of light exposure and most of all your own context without interpreting what you find beyond stating actual, exact observations about what is happening to you on a day to day basis.You have the option of opening a journal here.

You cannot expect something that was in the making for years to resolve in less than a week. 7 hours sleep is a good time. The timing of when it occurs is a gradual retraining process.
Clonazepam is not a sleeping aid even though it may have calming effects. If it is doctor prescribed I suggest you talk to the doc about what can be done.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wake-up-at-2am.23232/post-266871>

Not sure if this would help but I’ve had candida and biofilm issues for 3 yrs. I was insomniac as well. Always woke up in middle of night and go to the bathroom. It took me 3 yrs to fix this leaky gut. But living up here in the North doesn’t help with getting the sun during winters. But like [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) said getting enough sunlight helps. Every time Vancouver gets the sun in the spring I’m back stronger and I sleep right through the night. I finally brought myself a red light therapy and that helps tremendously.

### Kana — 2019-04-01T00:29:06+0300

Bruno,

I’m no doctor but you mentioned you have inflammations. From what I understand, any inflammation comes from your leaky gut. No I’ve not experienced vaginal nor intestinal candida. It was my gut. I’ve had Iga blood exam, stool test and also you can tell by looking at your tongue. If you have a whitish tongue than it’s candida. [@Dan2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20918/) may be right...my naturopath also practices TCM she mentioned gallbladder and liver functions. But candida really messes up your entire internal organs. Even if you’re doing everything right, it takes time. Also what I found was I used to take so much supplements it was like my immune system was on an overdrive. Your body is a incredible machine, it’ll heal by itself. I stopped taking my supplements and realized my healing went much more smoothly than taking these pills. I also practice intermittent fasting. That has helped with my candida issues too

Waking at 2 am might be the liver having difficulty. So maybe the fix would have to do with improving gallbladder function dealing with the fats at night somehow, as you seem to already be working on. Or maybe the gallbladder function is good enough for the next step with the liver and it's a separate issue with the liver that the improvement of wouldn't depend on first improving the gallbladder function. ### ElectricUniverse — 2019-05-21T18:40:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wake-up-at-2am.23232/post-270990>

As a seeker of optimal sleep (a fundamental prerequisite to optimal health and longevity) I know there can be times when most of us hit a wall in getting a good night's sleep for a multitude of reasons. An occasional dodgy sleep episode is no cause for alarm, but if it recurs more frequently a sleep deficit could begin to build-- and that is not good.

One significant cause, I believe, (but I've not undergone a medical sleep study) for occasional bouts of bad sleep in past are toxicity (liver, gall bladder, intestines, and other eliminative organs are sweating bullets during sleep to schlepp that sludge around your body to get it ready for excretion).

I've read that some of your body toxin load is continually seeping into intestines 24/7, including during nighttime. Ideally, this goo would be excreted in poop speedily with 2 or 3 bowel movements the next day. Alas, sometimes the toxins just get recirculated in _hepatic portal system_ and so they get another sightseeing tour of the body.

So going on the offensive with a good, ongoing detox program (be sensible about it-- there are many ways to do it wrong and potentially aggravate things) is a good start to improving sleep.

You already know better than to indulge in late day/evening snacking and food indulgences-- that practice will just gum up the eliminative organs during sleep and lead to disturbed sleep (and make you fatter).

You also know that you need to be in an EMF quiet zone to sleep well. If you have Wi-Fi running in your place (or next door neighbor), have a cell phone/smart device near your bed, or are unlucky enough to have cellular towers, smart meters, and antennae nearby then you are being buzzed and zapped during sleep with pulsating radiofrequency waves. Need I say more?

Dr Kruse's prescription for light therapy is key. Besides sun exposure, blocking blue light, and using red light you might want to investigate the use of green light therapy to help with your circadian cycle. It works on non-visual light receptors in brain and could help out (I haven't tried it yet). It is recommended to use true green light bulbs (dichromatic) and not the usual bulbs with a coating of green paint/pigments (or red, etc). As usual, do your due diligence with all hacking.

# Thread: Acute Diarrhea - solutions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-diarrhea-solutions.23237/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2019-03-04T11:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-diarrhea-solutions.23237/post-264417>

When you have acute diarrhea, say because of germs, bad water or rotten food, is there a api paleo solution for that?
Conventional advice is, not to eat fat and milk products and easy digestible food. Most of these food are of course not Epi-Paleo and are not fitting in the light cycle.
From my experience I do have no hunger in the beginning and fast naturally but eventually I get hungry and have to eat something, say after 24 hrs or so. When I eat normal (protein and fat) the diarrhea starts again.
Moro's carrot soup helps and gruel with grated appel, too. But certainly this is not Epi-Paleo.
I think this may be interesing for people who travel a lot to countries with bad water/food quality.

### Inger — 2019-03-04T16:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-diarrhea-solutions.23237/post-264430>

Maybe Iodine? Like a few drops of Lugols in water to kill the germs?

Another thing.. I guess there is a reason why the diarrhea is there, to remove the germs so maybe just let it run its course, and eat normally? Eventually it will stop.. Hydrating would be important.. and enough salt I guess. Oysters would be great because they are salty and full of minerals and very easy on digestion.

I have not had real diarrhea for so long.... if I ever have an issue it is just for a day or two and it goes away, I do not do anything special.

If it is prolonged diarrhea I would try to figure out what causes it....and fix that

Find the source,the cure will present itself. If the animal has a parasite either the animal will win or the parasite. Both is acceptable as far as balance is concerned. Some animals like dogs can recognise beneficial plants (by their light emission maybe) but we have lost that sense in captivity. Accute diarrhea kills if left untreated. So find the source. The question is not how do you get rid of it but why do you get it ? We do not live in the wild anymore so wild answers are problematic in an alien human modified environment.

### Inger — 2019-03-06T10:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acute-diarrhea-solutions.23237/post-264568>

Alex, my landlord and her 6 yo daughter have had really heavy diarrhea last days too! Seems to be some sort of virus maybe? The girl visited me several times as she was sick too... but I have not got any issues yet....

Animals in nature follow their instincts I guess.. So if you feel hungry, eat, and eat what you have appetite of .

I felt a little sick a week ago after working 2 very long and late days, some sort of flu I guess? But I had no couch or anything, just a little water dripping of my nose... but I felt a bit sick. So I just relaxed - naked - in the sun, (5 hours each day) for 2 days (I was luck we had fabulous weather!), and did nothing. Except I ate a lot! I really had appetite... so I ate lots of meat.. oysters.... I just craved food. Even if I was sick. I guess my body needed it, I always give in for cravings like that.

If you do not have appetite, it means your body wants to fast. Easy . The body is very clever I guess, we just have to listen

# Thread: 1,281 days: A journal of recovery from severe ME/CFS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hollyrebecca — 2019-03-04T22:21:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264465>

After two months of waning energy, strange neurological symptoms, and swollen glands, I finally collapsed on August 29th 2015. I had just graduated. It took me awhile to get a diagnosis, but when I finally did, the consultant said it was the most severe case of ME/CFS he had ever seen. I could not move any of my limbs and I could not speak. I could not comprehend more than three words at a time. I couldn’t tolerate touch and I had continual visual disturbances and a feeling of pressure in my skull. I also had severe light, noise, and movement sensitivity.

I was too ill to research anything, and I didn’t know what to do. I had no help. I lay in a dark room for two and half years. After 2 1/2 years I finally had a little bit of energy to speak. I used this energy to ask my sister to research things. It was a slow process. Around this time I caught a snippet of something on the radio downstairs in my parents house – the chief medical officer saying something about cases of fatigue increasing due to low vitamin D.

My mother had skin cancer when I was a child. I was paranoid about the sun. I wore sunglasses at all times and SPF even in the winter. Before getting ill my immunity was poor and I was particularly prone to urinary and sinus infections. My skin was bad. Both of my parents have auto-immune illnesses. My digestion was terrible. I was on the contraceptive pill for years.

I started taking vitamin D. 10,000iu a day. My muscle strength improved a little bit after a few months. I asked my mother if there was a way I could go outside. She bought me some dark wrap around sunglasses and, for the first time in years, I went into the sun. I stayed out there for about 15 minutes, and when I came in I was so disorientated that I couldn’t walk straight. But gradually, over the course of that summer, I began to stay out for a bit longer.

I hadn’t read anything about ‘primal’ living. I didn’t know it was a movement or anything, but all my instincts at this point were primal. I knew I needed light, sun, company. But it was so difficult because I was so sensitive to these things.

As I became stronger, I started to do my own research. I switched to a paleo diet and started taking key supplements such as, high dose B12, probiotics, vitamin K, and methylfolate, selenium, and iodine. I took Epsom salt baths and sunbathed whenever I could.

Now. I am perhaps 40% recovered. My muscles are stronger and I can cook. I’ve moved to the countryside and my partner grows fresh vegetables. My cognition has improved enough to read and write. I can go out 2 to 3 times a week, and these trips are BLISSFUL! I never thought I would leave my bedroom. I usually go to the woods, or to the beach. My sleep has improved and my mood is good. Asthma and allergies have disappeared.

This month I found this website. I think I got the captcha wrong three times in a row. The information here is dense and I’m still getting my head around it. I have started: grounding, getting AM light, wearing blue blocking glasses, eating seafood, and drinking spring water. In just a month I have noticed that my light sensitivity has decreased.

This illness is a vicious circle. The very things that make us better are difficult for people with CFS to access. By going very slowly I seem to be able to access these things, and I really hope that I can improve more. I’m learning a lot from you guys.

They’re gone ~ it’s an old photo! I’ll update it soon. I do still have to use sunglasses when I reach my light tolerance for the day, or I get migraines and cognitive problems. But gradually I am trying to increase the time without them, and I never wear them in the morning.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-05T18:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264517>

Best of luck to you. CFS is more widespread than you might think, and it is believed to have many co-factors contributing to morbidity.

There is a large body of research on CFS that has been accumulating over the years. Much of it points to causation by infections/immune dysfunction (mainly the usual suspects-- retroviruses- e.g. EBV and HHV-6, as well as genetically encoded endogenous retroviruses) and a predisposition due to a number of contributing factors, including genetics and environmental exposures. When the terrain is right, diseases come on in and make themselves at home.

Glad you found this forum on your healing journey.

Touch moves us strangely by design.

Is mother nature your mistress? If not, you best engage with her daily. Her game is all that matters in life, death, and love. She is the nurse that heals the infirmed of the mind and body.

The reason life can hurt us so much at times when we are ill is that we have separated our souls from the invisible forces nature wields over us and this forces us into a disconnected existence in life. This discomfort is how she touches you back wireless to give the information that your choices are not wise for you. What matters most is how well you walk through the fire of a challenge, with respect to disease.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T21:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264638>

I have the simplest tastes in life Holly. I am only satisfied with the best for me in this life. I won't settle, period. If you settle for what you've got, you deserve what you get. Is there a difference between giving into something and accepting it? I think so.......

Karma has no menu. You get served what you deserve based upon the choices you touch your life with.

Check the lights you allow around you.......you might be touched by wisdom. Nature always looks back at you.

We always see our worst selves. Our most vulnerable selves when we are infirmed. We need someone else to get close enough to tell us we’re wrong. Someone we trust.........this is why my mistress is nature. Behind her big blue eyes is the touch of a deft surgeon.

We are all wonderful, beautiful wrecks. That's what connects us--that we're all broken, all beautifully imperfect mosaics of stardust and light.

### Hollyrebecca — 2019-03-06T21:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264645>

I think I could come to Mexico. I could go anywhere. There is just enough madness in me (or sanity?). I said I’d do ANYTHING to get better and I meant it.

I’m on the screen too much. I’m isolated here. When people are around I interact and I’m happy, but I’m alone so much. Honestly once I felt grateful for the company of a fly. True story. It felt nice to have another living thing in the room.

I love that we are all mosaics of stardust and light. It’s beautiful and endlessly comforting.

I’ve been reading about more and more M.E. patients getting diagnosed with cranial instability. So far four patients have had surgery, and two have completely recovered from severe M.E. Over thirty more have been diagnosed with CCI in the past month.

The operation costs £70,000. Even the MRI costs £1,700.

Why would this happen to M.E. patients?

Could cranial instability be fixed any other way?

I’m continuing the Kruse protocol but I am still very ill and wondering if I need to shell out for an MRI.

Are there any particular bits of the protocol I should focus on?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-06-25T16:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-273256>

low solar redox = poor ligament and tendon and disc function.

Improve your ability to think = low solar redox too

-   -   Thinking is not IQ. When people talk about thinking they make the mistake of thinking that people with high IQs think better. That’s not what I’m talking about. I hate to break it to you but unless you’re trying to get into Mensa, IQ tests don’t matter as much as we think they do. After a certain point, that’s not the type of knowledge or brainpower that makes you better at life, happier, or more successful. It’s a measure sure, but a relatively useless one.

        If you want to outsmart people who are smarter than you, temperament and life-long learning are more important than IQ. Become a life long learner and do it under the power of the sun and the absence of the sun during night. Nothing works better or is as simple for the functioning of the human brain says this brain surgeon.

        Two of the guiding principles that Black Swans should follow on their path towards seeking wisdom are: (1) See the sunrise every AM and go to bed smarter than when you woke up; and (2) I’m not smart enough to figure everything out myself, so I want to ‘master the best of what "parts" of the truth other people have already figured out.’

        Acquiring wisdom is hard work. Learning how to think is tougher. It means sifting through the information, filtering the bull shit, and connecting it to a framework built around Nature's wisdom that you can use. A lot of people want to get their opinions from someone else who is selling a product or narrative. I know this because whenever anyone blurts out an opinion on social media I know is suspect and I ask why, I get some hastily re-phrased sound-byte that doesn’t contextualize the problem, identify the forces at play, demonstrate differences or similarities with previous situations, consider base rates, or … anything else that would demonstrate some level of thinking. One of my favorite questions to probe thinking is to ask what information would cause someone to change their mind. Immediately stop listening and leave if they say ‘I can’t think of anything.’ That is how I operate my circle of six. I want humans who know how to pack their own parachutes because they think well.
