Chris, welcome. What struck me was the new home. I think you might want to invest in one or two meters to identify where the nnEMF hotspots are. Or hire an expert to show you where you'll need to make adjustments. If you're trainer position is in a gym you'll have far less options to do anything about that but you could at least check how serious that environment is. There are tri-mode meters (measure RF, AC magnetic, and AC electric) like the TriField TF2, Acousticom 2, and ENV RD-10. For dirty electricity you would need something like the Stetzerizer microsurge meter. There are good online tutorials on how to use them.

This was also around the same time I stopped working outside which I am now doing again. I don’t know if my new home and job were to blame for this or if something intrinsically changed in this area. I am willing to take any steps necessary to gain optimal health here and if I cannot do that I will have to move.

### ChrisnJr — 2019-07-01T15:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-273533>

18 months since I first started to notice a decline in my health. 4 months since I discovered Dr Kruse’s teachings and started researching mitochondrial health. Since April 1st I have worked outside under the sun every day including most weekends. I’m lucky enough to have a job that is constantly outside usually in rural, Suburban areas and I am able to work without a shirt on which I do everyday. I make a point to watch the sunrise every morning. I spend 90 percent of my waking time Outdoors including exercising barefoot in my back yard 3-5 times a week followed by an ice cold shower.

Im doing the best I can with water, drinking mostly Voss and Aqua Panna from glass bottles. Eating 6-10 oysters and a half jar of Salmon roe every day. Along with raw milk, yogurt and butter, pastured eggs, Bone broth with coconut oil and mackerel for additional DHA. I have noticed an incredible improvement in my strength and body composition, I’ve lost almost 20 pounds and very lean while feeling as strong as ever.

I am however still plagued by stomach issues and mood swings/depression/lethargy. Sometimes I have to struggle just to get through my workday. The fatigue is like nothing I’ve ever felt. Not sure if I should be adding some seasonal carbs into the mix.

My digestion is still a mess most of the time.
Zero regularity, constantly belching and hiatus hernia symptoms. I am eating a huge breakfast then another large meal around 5 pm and that is it. Before my health problems I was a regular coffee consumer, 1-3 cups daily but I have almost cut it out completely. Ironically the coffee used to help boost my mood and energy while also aiding digestion but I realize how ignorant I was to be using it as a crutch.

I was working with a functional medicine practitioner before I discovered this forum who tells me I have mold toxicity causing my chronic fatigue and constant gas/stomach problems but I know Dr kruse’s take on all that. I feel like I am doing everything I can from a quantum perspective. Also blue blocking every night and sleeping in total darkness with the WiFi off. I am astounded by how much stronger and leaner I have become but I would almost trade those benefits for quality digestion and great energy. My family and peers look at me and assume that I must feel great is back but sadly that is not yet the case.

I am extremely thankful to be a part of this group and having discovered this information I have definitely seen the value. I have always felt a strong connection to nature and now more than ever I have hope which is something I could not always say over the past 18 months. If anyone can offer any insight as always thank you in advance

### Zehlersan — 2026-02-23T10:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-367439>

If you do need something while eating try a sparkling mineral water it's usually more acidic helping aid in digestion. Warm water and half a lemon 1hr before a meal can boost acidity as well, or 1-2oz of water with a 1/8th teaspoon of cayenne pepper 1hr before meal does the same. I've learned the last 2 remedies mentioned from Barbara O'Neil. I hope this helps. Blessings to you brother on your journey.

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-09T05:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264868>

With the gut issues, when I need to get myself back on track, I find it easiest to fast for a few days and then I can start back on food with a very strict and disciplined program. You can use C8 coconut oil for energy as it goes straight to your liver. Eat simple and put as much time between meals as possible. Whenever your get isn't working it has an opportunity to heal. No sugar, No sweeteners, low carb.
I live in Iowa and I think in the north we have to be a lot more intentional about getting sunlight. I spent 1 month this winter down south in the sun and my Vitamin D is only a 39, with normal range being 30-100. Have you read the Leptin Rx? Are you doing any cold therapy?

Thank you, I’m reading the Leptin RX now. I’m wondering if CT is necessary for me this time of year since I’m working outside in the cold all day. I will definitely be adding this to my life once it gets warmer outside.

### drezy — 2019-03-10T00:39:17+0200

> [ChrisnJr said:](/goto/post?id=264908)
>
> Thank you, I’m reading the Leptin RX now. I’m wondering if CT is necessary for me this time of year since I’m working outside in the cold all day. I will definitely be adding this to my life once it gets warmer outside.

### caroline — 2019-03-10T00:56:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264956>

I think, personally, that C/T is more necessary in the winter ...... become a great white shark = cold dark and boring!

what is your outside job?

There is a guide for C/T .....and a huge C/T thread.....dig in!

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-10T05:56:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264991>

Thanks for the encouragement on C/T Caroline
I have to agree with Chris that the idea of cold therapy is a mental hurdle when it is already cold all day, but my body is slowly adapting and the results are amazing. One thing that I really notice is more distinct circadian cycles, when I am asleep I sleep better, and when I am awake I am more alert and have better energy.
The C/T will make it much easier to be out in the cold working. Just start slowly then when it warms up you will be able to ramp up more easily.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-09T17:36:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-the-light-diet-begin.23267/post-264910>

It is not how Darwin or Watson or Crick envisioned.

few million years of chilly water and sunlight in the photic zone of the sea and you get new genes not passed on from mom or Dad. That is the basis of my OSF 3 blog. Another prediction checked off. Here’s the quantum evolution of a new gene from non-coding DNA, step by step. It's a gene for antifreeze in Arctic fish. This should be very interesting for neo-Darwinists because it destroys the narrative of Watson and Crick. It is the 1st time molecular biology has to admit that Lamarck's ideas are not quackery. It shows the definitive truth that de novo gene creation EXISTS in life. It has to make the curious out there wonder what other quantum functions non-coding genes have that we are unaware of? #BlackSwanwisdom The colder the water the more DHA is in the seafood............think that matters too? YEP. [https://www.pnas.org/content/116/10/4400](https://www.pnas.org/content/116/10/4400) …

# Thread: Thought Experiment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-10T03:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/post-264971>

The above video is of a light bulb powered by what I believe is **electromagnetism**. I will test my knowledge (after googling for the last bit) by trying to explain how the light bulb turns on...and then relate it to quantum biology.

Water - In this case tap water I assume, fundamentally made of H20 **molecules** (**atomic** structures of 2 hydrogens attached to one oxygen).

Add salt: Dare I say, a "phenomenon" occurs whereby the salt (sodium chloride) mixed with H20 forms an **electrolyte** substrate conducive to **electrical current** (electrical current or electricity being either **electrons** or **ion** flow) in this case, ion flow.

The wire that is stripped at one end forms the **electrodes** (two different materials that relatively attract positive (**protons**) or negative (electrons)). As the electrodes touch water, the positive electrode attracts the negatively charged Chloride (Cl-) which recieved a transfer of electrons while in solution) and the negative electrode attracts the Na+ (positive charged sodium which has given up an electron in solution). The cross travel of the ions (Na+, Cl-) creates electricity ,which in itself, I believe would power the light bulb, by completing the circuit (basically chloride is carrying the extra electron from one electrode to the other), hence electrical current?

We have our battery!

This is the part that is a bit confusing for me. The 3 conditions whereby **magnets (**IE special orderly material that has its own energy fields - magnetic fields) are when the magnetic and electric fields are variably , OR magnetic field is variable and electrical field constant, OR electrical field variable and magnetic field constant.

In this case, electrical field is variable? as the electrolyte is moving ions/electrons. I believe the magnet is boosting the current here .

EDIT: ADD BELOW.. Sorry I accidentally posted premature.

So then I guess electro magnetic fields are basically a phenomenon whereby electrical and magnetic fields COMBINE to create super energy? lol IE the electro magnetic spectrum.

APPLICATION:

The ocean versus fresh water. Salt water is more conductive. I'm guessing from a quantum biology perspective, with metals in us, hemoglobin made from iron, etc, there is magnetism and thus we are electrically charging ourselves like the light bulb in water (crude crude connection). Add in light (which is part of the electromagnetic spectrum) i'm starting to see that energy transfer (movement of energy, electrons), is the spice, the key, IS life. SUN emits electromagnetic energy ,stored ideally in ocean water, we receive this energy via water AND OR sunlight.

Its starting to make sense.. if only very crudely.

\*\*FEEL FREE TO CORRECT ANY OF THIS LANGUAGE, I KNOW I DONT HAVE IT CORRECT FOR SURE\*\*\*

J

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-10T04:13:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/post-264977>

Light is an electromagnetic wave that always has its electric field and magnetic field orthogonal to one another.......uneless man fucks it up and engineers it like they have with 5G.

### Allin — 2019-03-11T01:30:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-265057>

> [ChrisnJr said:](/goto/post?id=265027)
>
> Is it ok to read a book at night with a red light head lamp such as one could purchase at a hardware store ?

If I can assume you see sunrise every morning, have the power grid to your house turned off at night, have a great redox and want to use a red light to read at night for an hour or two before you go to sleep at 9 or 10 PM instead of a candle, fire or kerosene lamp - I am sure it is okay.

### Mike David — 2019-03-11T16:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-265118>

> [Kathy Spears said:](/goto/post?id=265110)
>
> When I read about the power grid being turned off at night, I'm trying to figure out the best way to accomplish this. Do I just go to the breaker box and turn off everything for each bedroom? I need an alarm clock (not using cell!!) so I'm not sure how to do this. I've seen the equipment you can buy that is like a zone kill switch...
> So much to figure out! It would be easier if I had even a little understanding of electricity!

Have someone go into the room you want killed. You go to the breaker box and start flipping the single 15 or 20 amp ones (numbers are on the breaker handle) until the room goes off. Put a sticker on the ones you want off then repeat the process yourself every night. Don’t forget when you wake up that it’s your fault the powers out not electric company. Make sure you turn off sensitive electronic equipment before throwing breakers on and off. If your lucky each breaker should be labeled on the cover door. Make sure you didn’t cut off the fridge, sump pump, heater ect... along with your bedroom.

### Mike David — 2019-03-12T00:12:07+0200

I would kill as many circuits as possible and sleep as far away from the electrical box and meter and neighbors setups too. Then buy the one that meausures low and high emf energy and skip the magnetic effect detector. I would just buy a magnetic electrical field detector as well. If your swimming in 5G you need a volt meter too to measure stray skin voltages. Or the best option would be sleep On the earth like a real mammal.

### Charyam — 2019-10-14T12:30:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-280396>

Don't forget that there may be lighting cables:-
a)Under the bed which light the room underneath (if you aren't in a bungalow)
b) Above the bed.

I removed the cables under the bed when doing some house remodelling.

Whilst these may not be a problem when sleeping, I thought it good to shift the cables to a point further away from chairs and settees.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-10-24T18:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-281179>

Here's a new one for Dr. Jack Kruse "I told you so" archives.
Blue Light at night (bALAN) raises blood glucose, alters insulin response and also increases hunger in DIURNAL rodents.
"Diurnal" means these animals in the study are naturally active during the daytime like humans and not active at nighttime (nocturnal) like the vast majority of rodents used in studies. So this is an important study because it relates more closely to you (diurnal humans).
Start taking artificial light at night seriously folks. You may think those weird colored glasses from RaOptics that some of us are wearing at night are funny but it is certainly no joke to your long term health.
[https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.14814/phy2.14257](https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.14814/phy2.14257)

# Thread: Bed Canopy ?  Silver / foil ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jackie Nelson — 2019-03-10T21:28:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/post-265039>

Listened to a wonderful podcast with Jack Kruse and Luke Story and Jack mentioned that "tin foil"
could be used to block out EMF coming in windows. Am wondering if tin foil (or those low cost foil camping
blankets) could be made into a bed canopy to block EMF's I've seen the silver fabric canopy's and
might get one of those but curious if aluminum foil would also work ? Any comments appreciated!

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-11T17:13:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/post-265119>

> [Jackie Nelson said:](/goto/post?id=265039)
>
> Listened to a wonderful podcast with Jack Kruse and Luke Story and Jack mentioned that "tin foil"
> could be used to block out EMF coming in windows. Am wondering if tin foil (or those low cost foil camping
> blankets) could be made into a bed canopy to block EMF's I've seen the silver fabric canopy's and
> might get one of those but curious if aluminum foil would also work ? Any comments appreciated!

That was an insightful podcast with Uncle Jack and Story.

Yes, those so-called '_emergency blankets_' (aluminum-coated mylar sheets) certainly seem to be shielding adequately up to 4G-- but who knows how effective they will be in a 5G world. Heck, even aluminum cooking foil works in 4G.

The problem with bed canopies is that, to be truly safe, (or at least as safe as possible) you have to employ not only the top canopy but also shield sides and bottom of bed. Radiation is likely from any angle due to the plethora of antennas and transmitters these days in urban environments.

If you have a sizeable leak or opening in your canopy setup incoming RF could possibly enter and ricochet around you, increasing exposure. Only by using a good RF meter can you rationally validate your RF mitigation efforts.

Fabric shield canopies work well because they allow plenty of ventilation inside. Foils do not. Also, foil materials are conductive and will need grounding to work properly.

Before investing a lot of money in shielding fabric canopies in this incipient 5G world you should pin down the seller explicitly about how effective they will be against 5G frequencies, and especially the small wave forms like millimeter waves. Size of fabric weave apertures is key.

### Sun Disciple — 2019-03-11T18:27:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/post-265130>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=265119)
>
> That was an insightful podcast with Uncle Jack and Story.
>
> Yes, those so-called '_emergency blankets_' (aluminum-coated mylar sheets) certainly seem to be shielding adequately up to 4G-- but who knows how effective they will be in a 5G world. Heck, even aluminum cooking foil works in 4G.
>
> The problem with bed canopies is that, to be truly safe, (or at least as safe as possible) you have to employ not only the top canopy but also shield sides and bottom of bed. Radiation is likely from any angle due to the plethora of antennas and transmitters these days in urban environments.
>
> If you have a sizeable leak or opening in your canopy setup incoming RF could possibly enter and ricochet around you, increasing exposure. Only by using a good RF meter can you rationally validate your RF mitigation efforts.
>
> Fabric shield canopies work well because they allow plenty of ventilation inside. Foils do not. Also, foil materials are conductive and will need grounding to work properly.
>
> Before investing a lot of money in shielding fabric canopies in this incipient 5G world you should pin down the seller explicitly about how effective they will be against 5G frequencies, and especially the small wave forms like millimeter waves. Size of fabric weave apertures is key.

None of the sellers can possibly validate claims of 5g mitigation.

### Jackie Nelson — 2019-03-12T03:22:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/post-265179>

Thank you both for your responses. Appreciated. Yes, it is difficult to know whether the costly silver mesh canopies will be of any use in 5G.
I also heard Jack say that if your area has announced that 5G is coming then you have probably already had it for 2-3 years because they test
it for that long. Here in Nelson, NZ we've been told it will be here in 2020, so have probably already been exposed to it. I tried making a foil
blanket canopy but as you said there is not enough ventilation because there is no air flow as there would be through the silver mesh.
On another topic - there have been predictions of epidemic suicides from 5G and my best friends son just took his own life and this is the 4th
suicide that has impacted me in the past year or so, before that, NOTHING. GET SUNSHINE, GROUND, BLOCK BLUE LIGHT, GET AWAY FROM as much EMF's as possible.
Tell your loved ones. tell everyone!

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-13T18:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bed-canopy-silver-foil.23275/post-265368>

> [Sun Disciple said:](/goto/post?id=265130)
>
> None of the sellers can possibly validate claims of 5g mitigation.

I think as 5G awareness grows consumers will be pointedly asking these questions of vendors, and they better have some pretty convincing answers.

# Thread: I healed and it had nothing to do with emf or nature PART 2
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-healed-and-it-had-nothing-to-do-with-emf-or-nature-part-2.23279/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7

### Julian — 2019-03-11T13:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-healed-and-it-had-nothing-to-do-with-emf-or-nature-part-2.23279/post-265088>

As the last thread got closed i am continuing on with my new found discovery and relationship with god outside of duality/logic in mans failed attempt to understand the infinite. In my last thread i said that none of this stuff actually worked as i tried it all and it wasn't until i completely submitted to god as a individual who exists outside of nature/logic that i started to heal. That being said i do now believe that momentary truths that exist in nature are only accessible and truly work when in a relationship to god through love and faith. Everything outside of that which works is the placebo effect with ego/false belief in the self. If the self belief does work it is not permanent and is essentially what the bible describes to be Satan "the master counterfeiter". Jack's logical truths are fundamental but still only momentary in a sense and they will forever be hypothesis as the only true scientific truth is that truth is changeable. I still strongly believe in living by the very deep momentary logic that jack has uncovered but i also strongly suggest people find a relationship to god outside of logic so then the attempts at utilizing the momentary logic will become much more realized as in my case. I also personally still think that a plant based diet is more of a fundamental truth then any animal based diet but i have zero scientific/logical proof for that. I only believe that unconditional love for all things and not being involved in something that causes the pain and suffering of other sentient beings is a fundamental truth of growth/life.

Jack don't close this post bro its just a neutral conversation and i am still promoting your beliefs

### Julian — 2019-03-12T03:32:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-healed-and-it-had-nothing-to-do-with-emf-or-nature-part-2.23279/post-265180>

I am not saying you have to call your god by the same name i do but you do have to give it a name to help individualize it as a being outside of space/time/matter/nature. He needs be a single entity that watches what you do otherwise your moral conscious is subject to never ending self interpretation of what is right and wrong based on god being your self based subjective interpretation. If god is nature then where does the moral compass come from? Without a definitive answer its just continual states of chaos, truths and untruths just like nature. How you come to have a relationship and personalize your god will be different then mine. It also should be primarily based around one of the main religions of the world as these religions are well grounded in universally agreed apon moral goodness and truths. The further you stray into more minority sub sects the more subjective moral freedom and logic starts to blur the lines of fundamental truth. That being said anyone can have a solid understanding and relationship to god in any position.

A star explodes in a distant galaxy and millions of years later parts of it affect your biology. You have the power to affect everything in the universe by this god given entanglement. The trick the “devil” pulled is making you think you have to submit something “god” to have access to the power. It was given to you when sperm met egg. Surf with nature and nature will have you realize your otherworldly abilities. The devil is nothing but your neocortex holding you back. Devil=the god in your neocortex blinding you from the absolute mastery of nature. A fluctuating vibrating electrical waveform, connecting affecting building destroying all matter in the universe at the same time. Nature=you=god all for one and one for all every atom plays its crucial part.

# Thread: PEER review fallacy persists.......and will be used to control you eventually.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peer-review-fallacy-persists-and-will-be-used-to-control-you-eventually.23283/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-11T15:05:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peer-review-fallacy-persists-and-will-be-used-to-control-you-eventually.23283/post-265098>

As debates continue about citing preprints vs 'peer-reviewed' papers, important to note that there are other experiments that undermine the \[flawed\] premise that peer-reviewed = established = citable

The reviewers do not believe the biophysics because they do not even know it is physically possible and plausible in biology for this to happen. Is it no wonder why our chronic disease metric grow as the experts sit by and argue about things they are DEAD wrong about?

The public good and health are being destroyed by how we do science today.

Reviewers found this not believable and technically flawed. Yet e-life chose to publish it. I agree with that decision to publish it and then let people dissect the paper themselves without the interference of the editors of PEER panel. If the paper sucks we'll figure it out. This is how public reviews of restaurants close bad restaurants fast in New Orleans. Not publishing controversial papers buries critical gems found in labs.

Of course, the idea that all peer-reviewed literature is perfect is not true. But the expert scientist will fight hard on social media to tell the public it is reliable because it is part of their lifeblood and career. They are protecting their turf, but fail to realize this turf is OWNED by the PUBLIC who pays for this research. We have copious amounts of evidence it is horribly flawed. But just because it doesn't work perfectly, doesn't mean it's not working some of the time or the majority of the time. Remember that scientists are NOT the only users of the scientific literature!

Press folks, reporters, funding agencies (not necessarily govt), patients, policymakers may not appreciate the caveats or read the reviewers' comments. I'm not convinced this improves the perceived credibility of the literature. IMO, editors should not be gatekeepers in science that is new or cutting edge. Their biases limit the production of innovative science experiments.

The whole point of that e-Life journal experiment was that ALL papers sent out to be peer reviewed would be published with reviews, good or bad. This is the way things should work. The challenge is to get the mechanics right and to show people how & why it’s better for the public.

Regulating G protein-coupled receptors by topological inversion was the paper.

[https://elifesciences.org/articles/40234](https://elifesciences.org/articles/40234)

Why do I support the publishing of the paper?

None of the idiot scientist critiquing this review process on social media understands how 1G-5G cause topologic inversion.......and this paper shows its blood and guts of how the process can be broken in cells by nnEMF. The paper is flawed but the mechanism is so clear of how topologic changes induce downstream effects in cells that will lead to emergent diseases and symptoms in many patients shocking patients and doctors.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This topic of topology is going to be a critical understanding of any Black Swan soon.........It is why Kawasaki's disease and measles are being confused in ER's all over America now and Big Pharma will use the confusion to push for mandatory vaccines. Know one see the end game until they feel it in their biology.

**This is a wake-up call kind of paper that reminds of this passage from Atlas Shrugged.**

“I quit when medicine was placed under State control, some years ago,” said Dr. Hendricks. “Do you know what it takes to perform a brain operation? Do you know the kind of skill it demands, and the years of passionate, merciless, excruciating devotion that go to acquire that skill? That was what I would not place at the disposal of men whose sole qualification to rule me was their capacity to spout the fraudulent generalities that got them elected to the privilege of enforcing their wishes at the point of a gun. I would not let them dictate the purpose for which my years of study had been spent, or the conditions of my work, or my choice of patients, or the amount of my reward. I observed that in all the discussions that preceded the enslavement of medicine, men discussed everything—except the desires of the doctors. Men considered only the ‘welfare’ of the patients, with no thought for those who were to provide it. That a doctor should have any right, desire, or choice in the matter, was regarded as irrelevant selfishness; his is not to choose, they said, only ‘to serve.’ That a man who’s willing to work under compulsion is too dangerous a brute to entrust with a job in the stockyards—never occurred to those who proposed to help the sick by making life impossible for the healthy.”

“I have often wondered at the smugness with which people assert their right to enslave me, to control my work, to force my will, to violate my conscience, to stifle my mind—yet what is it that they expect to depend on when they lie on an operating table under my hands? Their moral code has taught them to believe that it is safe to rely on the virtue of their victims. Well, that is the virtue I have withdrawn. Let them discover the kind of doctors that their system will now produce. Let them discover, in their operating rooms and hospital wards that it is not safe to place their lives in the hands of a man whose life they have throttled. It is not safe if he is the sort of many who resents it—and still less safe if he is the sort who doesn’t.”

Another interesting passage from Atlas Shrugged deeply related to recent conversations with several friends of mine in the medical profession.

# Thread: Pros/cons camper life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2019-03-11T22:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-265160>

What are the pros/cons to living in a camper for 8 months with my 3 kids? It'd be small... gotta be able to tow it with my mini van. We obviously can't bring a lot of "stuff" which is good actually. We'll obviously spend most of our time outside. I love the idea of being able to move about and try some different spots in FL. Currently this seems like the easiest option to get me in the sun this coming winter to heal. Obviously the new campers on the market are LED intense with wifi boosters and nonsense, but I need a cheap used one anyway.

### nonchalant — 2019-03-12T00:36:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-265171>

I would keep your phone in the minivan, especially if the trailer has a metal roof/sides. Turned off at night, of course.
We have had some great times in our little trailer. I'll wake up feeling so refreshed.
In parks, watch out for some sites that have transformers sitting nearby. We have additional automotive shades for the windows, attaching them with velcro at night, including the roof vent and skylight over the shower.
A site that is away from outdoor lights but also has a view to the east is awesome.

### LieselK — 2019-03-12T01:39:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-265176>

Great points [@nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/)

My husband is least keen on the camper idea because of safety for me and kids. He is also concerned about his ability to talk/skype with us regularly.

### caroline — 2019-03-12T03:54:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-265184>

Great idea! It will be absolutely fantastic for you and the kids. I am in Oz .....everyone here \[pretty much\] has a camper of some description and does a big lap around Oz. People are on the road for years with kids and home school them

You can always go to a near by town to Skype. We often use Information centres.....for toilets and Skype.

town swimming pools are a great way to get showers etc.

### Jackie Nelson — 2019-03-12T04:00:45+0200

Nelson that was in the middle of the downtown area, concrete all around, with strong Wi-Fi. Who the heck wants to "freedom" camp in
the middle of a busy parking lot? Used to be you could stop at some lovely waterfront area and enjoy nature, now they expect you to
park in a cement paved EMF hell hole. No thank you!

> Just curious why you’re headed down so early? North Carolina is bloody hot in Sept and even into Oct, and Florida will of course be much more so. Is it to keep continuity for the home schooling ?

Cause Jack said so.

But really, I think it's because of the length of day and avoiding so much transition with that. He had said that Apr 15-Aug 15 may only be optimal here in NC for ME. So I'm already fudging those dates a little.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-20T19:25:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266048>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=266045)
>
> New phenomena.... It phosphexifies it.

Oooooh—I like that!!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-20T19:34:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266050>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266047)
>
> Cause Jack said so.
>
> But really, I think it's because of the length of day and avoiding so much transition with that. He had said that Apr 15-Aug 15 may only be optimal here in NC for ME. So I'm already fudging those dates a little.

Hmmm. Well Jack probably does know best, but I’m wondering if the potential stress on your mental health is worth it? I just picture trying to manage everything outside in the buggy, muggy, sticky blech of Florida heat... (I’ve lived there before.) Jack does say we need to abandon our comfort zones though.

### LieselK — 2019-03-20T19:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266053>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=266050)
>
> Hmmm. Well Jack probably does know best, but I’m wondering if the potential stress on your mental health is worth it? I just picture trying to manage everything in the buggy, muggy, sticky blech of Florida heat... Jack does say we need to abandon our comfort zones though.

It does sound miserable, but it's already miserable here still in Sept with the heat and the bugs. Lean into the discomfort and the stress I guess. To put things in perspective though, we moved from OH to NC when I was 6 months pregnant with #3- it was July 4th weekend. It was pretty much like going through hell. I didn't know anyone here either and was preparing for a homebirth. My oldest started Kindergarten that fall and my middle son was a 3 year old hellion. The birth was difficult and my new baby cried ALL THE FREAKING TIME. Like always. For hours straight every evening especially. I have PTSD from it all. Then we moved into our actual home a year later (July 4th weekend AGAIN) with an 8 mo old that screamed bloody murder in the car all the time (at least outside of the car he was better!). These years have been the most stressful of my life while also watching my health decline and felt like things were slipping away. Since starting protocols last year and immediately seeing results I do what I need to do to be well. If I'm not well then who in my household is? If this is what I need to do to help me then I'll do it- any which way it will be stressful to some degree. More so probably for my husband this time around.

### Christine_L — 2019-03-20T23:18:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266061>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266038)
>
> I'm going to FL with my 3 kids Sept to April. My husband will stay in NC and work, but come visit and hopefully spend most of Dec with us. We can't afford anything too extravagant. I'm still looking into campers (we'd have to rent a truck to haul one down) and have found a couple under $5,000 that could work. Seems like the going rate for monthly sites at RV camps are around $550/mo and some have cabins or campers you can rent for at least $1,000/mo. I'm also looking for families that might allow us to stay in exchange for childcare or farm work. It's hard to find those ppl when I don't know many ppl in FL though. I've been joining FB pages and trying to ask around.

Wow! That takes a lot of courage, you are doing the right thing. If you need a bed for each person, have you looked into mobile homes? Otherwise this site seems alright. Not sure how much you have to spend. [https://www.rvtrader.com/rvs/rvs-for-sale?sleepingCapacity=4](https://www.rvtrader.com/rvs/rvs-for-sale?sleepingCapacity=4)

### Christine_L — 2019-03-20T23:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266062>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=266035)
>
> You'd put a flower in your hair I think. You could also wear Nancy Sinatra boots.
>
> (Just writing that, my entangled state can feel an incoming "These boots are made for walking" video link from [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) any second now...)

Yes to the flower in my hair! Maybe to the boots unless its hot and then its barefoot or Earthrunners for me

### Corey Nelson — 2019-03-21T18:44:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266124>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266038)
>
> I'm going to FL with my 3 kids Sept to April. My husband will stay in NC and work, but come visit and hopefully spend most of Dec with us. We can't afford anything too extravagant. I'm still looking into campers (we'd have to rent a truck to haul one down) and have found a couple under $5,000 that could work. Seems like the going rate for monthly sites at RV camps are around $550/mo and some have cabins or campers you can rent for at least $1,000/mo. I'm also looking for families that might allow us to stay in exchange for childcare or farm work. It's hard to find those ppl when I don't know many ppl in FL though. I've been joining FB pages and trying to ask around.

This is an awesome plan. I am similarly inspired by your post, as an NC resident...gotta add a shielded camper for FL trips to my goals list.

The site rental fee is crazy expensive, though, IMO. I like your barter idea better, and you could add some additional cool features to the camper (including some that might allow you to be more comfortable without a fully hooked up site) if you don't have to account for the monthly expense.

### LieselK — 2019-03-21T19:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266128>

> [Corey Nelson said:](/goto/post?id=266124)
>
> This is an awesome plan. I am similarly inspired by your post, as an NC resident...gotta add a shielded camper for FL trips to my goals list.
>
> The site rental fee is crazy expensive, though, IMO. I like your barter idea better, and you could add some additional cool features to the camper (including some that might allow you to be more comfortable without a fully hooked up site) if you don't have to account for the monthly expense.

The camper definitely wins on cost. I can find sites for less than $500/mo whereas any rentals (cabins at RV parks included) are at \*least\* $1,200/mo. Many want to charge me extra per night for any more than 2 people. Most RV sites don't do that- they tend to allow 4 people per site before charging more.

This research is sucking the life out of me these days. Where is my sunshine?! All the more reason to NOT be here next March.

There are def a lot of cool options on [www.workaway.info](http://www.workaway.info) if you just need a place for yourself- especially if you're at all handy to offer up a trade for lodging.

### caroline — 2019-03-22T09:26:09+0200

### caroline — 2019-03-24T02:43:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pros-cons-camper-life.23285/post-266312>

We all need to fake it till we make it! Tough is good, scared is good - been there many times! ....think of the great example you are setting for your kiddos.

Now you really know that you are alive! You really get how important this all is and you have skin in the game!

Your life - your dime .....make it work!

I just read this yesterday .....When a door closes - just open it again - it is only a door and that is what it is meant to do!

There are so many possibilities - I know it doesn't seem that way now .... you are at the starting gate of a brand new life and it will unfold in spectacular fashion.

Jack has been thru many changes and locations \[dragging his family kicking and screaming\] but he just keeps pushing forward and pursuing his dreams.

I thing one big trait we need is to always be adaptable and positive. Put a huge smile on your face and reach for the moon ..and then reach further. You really have nothing to lose! and everything to gain.......

# Thread: GLOSSARY OF TERMS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glossary-of-terms.23286/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-12T02:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glossary-of-terms.23286/post-265177>

Below is going to be a living document of terms that I will compile as I learn about them, for my own learning and hopefully for yours

**5G - in the context of our forum, it means "fifth generation" and relates to our cell phone reception. Relates to how quick we receive and texts via "cellular" route. Not to be confused with 5Ghz wifi, which is simply wifi at 5ghz, different. Similar, in the sense that wifi and cellular are two different ways that "waves" of energy packed with information travel through air to transmit information from person to person via our phones, computers (text, photos, audio, video), BUT different in that they operate on different "styles" of waves. EG. Moguls on the ski hill versus the green blue black runs. Maybe a bad example, but there are different ways to send "information" with "ELECTRIC-MAGNET" energy. Anyways, both bad, but it would appear that 5G (5th generation CELLULAR) is REALLY BAD NEWS.**

**ATOM - small piece of matter comprised of a collection of electrons, protons, neutrons. Refers to a neutral charged particle.

BLUE LIGHT - referring to the unhealthy indoor lighting in the context of our modern era. we need natural sunlight for a healthy life as the sun contains all types of colours of light (the "full spectrum or range", each with different energies (wavelengths and frequencies) in a right proportion for life on earth. our modern indoor environment, with home LED lights/phones/computers/tvs/ especially, not only takes us away from the full spectrum balanced light of the sun, but concentrates an imbalanced exposure to the light spectrum. Namely, blue light is harmful because it is very stimulating and messes up our circadian rhytmns (our natural ebbs and flows to the day, sleeping time , waking time etc). it suppresses Melatonin, which is normally released at bedtime naturally to help you sleep after the sun goes down in the day. blue light = less proper sleep = less proper body healing during sleep = more health problems.

CARBOHYDRATES - molecular arrangement of atoms such that its main evolutionary purpose is to create "fuel"or energy for the body to utilize.

CYTOCHROME - SIMILAR TO HEMOGLOBIN/MYOGLOBIN IN THAT IT CONTAINS HEME + A PROTEIN. KEY DIFFERENCE IS THAT ITS EVOLVED JOB IS TO JUST BE A "SHORT DISTANCE PASSER" OF ELECTRONS FROM ONE MOLECULE TO ANOTHER. WHEREAS HEMOGLOBIN, IS DESIGNED TO "HOLD TO ELECTRONS" TO PASS LONG DISTANCES WITHOUT LOSING/GIVING UP THE ELECTRONS. BASICALLY LIKE A SHORT OR LONG DISTANCE DELIVERY DRIVER.**

**ELECTRICITY - electrons moving. its what powers a light bulb, powers a human. Electrons moving in a loop so to speak.**

**ELECTROMAGNETISM - a word that describes how electricity + magnetism work...how they work together like two conjoined twins.

ELECTROMAGNETIC RADIATION - describes electricity and magnets moving through time and space

ELECTROMAGNETIC SPECTRUM - describes the different ways that electricity/magnets move, or radiate. It's energy, force, has an ability to power and affect change. And that energy or force travels in quanta, or little bite size cookie pieces. And those bite size cookies have an assortment of flavours based on certain characteristics. They move like waves of the ocean. Some are more like tidal waves, some more like smooth waves (amplitude). Some ripple, some are calm (frequency). These characteristics relate to how much "impact" each wave can make...IE.. how much energy or force its packing, how much it can "push through" other physical matter throughout space.**

**ELECTRON - subatomic particle that is intrinsically negatively charged. In other words, an arbitrary? "charge" that means it is attracted to an also arbitrary charge, or proton. BASICALLY electrons and protons are two pieces of physical matter that happen to be the ying and the yang, opposites that "attract." The significance is that this is the part of the ying and yang that, like a electrical circuit, so too in our bodies allow the "circuit" to function at a quantum level.

EMF - electromagnetic frequency. Electricity and Magnetism. The ying and the yang so to speak, in the sense that the are forever linked to each other. Where one is, the other exists. EMF is different LEVELS, styles ,brands, of the same thing. You got blue jeans, black jeans, jeans with holes, jeans that are stained, whatever. But they are all jeans. electromagnetic "frequency" then is just altering that electricity along with the magnetism that comes along with it...and altering the "waves" or the style of jeans. The significance? The tendency is that them racy short short jean shorts that girls where can be akin to shorter magentic frequency, in that they produce a larger "reaction," they travel through air and through HUMANS, materials, whatever, better, because its packed with more ENERGY. The longer waves don't = less energy. Doesn't mean one is good, one is bad. I think it just means its a different brand of jeans with pros and cons to the human body. ??**

**GLOBIN - "to contain a protein." EG HEMO-GLOBIN.. A HEME MOLECULE THAT CONTAINS A PROTEIN...**

**HEME - an arrangement of atoms such that the center of the molecular structure is an atom of iron surrounded by an arrangemnt of other molecules like 'ring" shaped arrangement. It is not a protein, nor a carbohydrate, as its too reduced to be considered either. Kind of like a computer room is not a "house" but rather just a room, and thus cannot be considered as such. Such an arangement is considered a "porphyrin structure", which is just a name for the particular arrangement.**

**HEMOGLOBIN - HEME + GLOBIN. THE COMBINATION OF HEME AND GLOBIN IN A MOLECULAR STRUCTURE. FOUND IN BLOOD, UNLIKE MYOGLOBIN, SAME THING FOUND IN MUSCLES**

**LIGHT - refers to electricity + magnets, electromagnetism. It describes a certain brand of electromagnetism that has a particular frequency/amplitude, or way its waves ripple. Also happens to be called, the visible spectrum in the electromagnetic spectrum, IE referring to how humans just happen to be able to see this part of the spectrum.**

**NEUTRON - a subatomic particle that forms an atom, alongside electrons and protons. Note neutrons are "neutr-al" or rather they dont have a charge and there is no ying and yang here.**

**Native versus Nonnative - (EMF) I think this refers to natural versus unnatural. Nature versus man made. Native = SUN. Native = earth. Nonnative = our microwaves, our cell phones, our computers, our light bulbs. The significance? We were evolved, we were born on this earth due to perfect conditions ... water...sunlight...etc. We were evolved, and adapted for natural sunlight, and its natural blend of electro magnetic frequencies. NON NATIVE emf is like a cheap, dummed down version. It's like eating salt, eating raw caco on it's own. Bitter. Wierd. Gross. But if you put salt and chocolate on a full cake, it hits the spot moreso. Same idea. NNEMF (non native emf) is the salt that just doesn't agree with our body because it doesnt have the full stuff. NEMF (native emf) does.**

**ION - refers to an ATOM or MOLECULE with a charge..either too many electrons to push it to the "negative side" or too many protons to push it to the "positive side" . An imbalance in ying and yang..resulting in an "unstable" or "reactive" atom/molecule that has more tendency to collide and alter and impact other atoms/molecules, etc. Consider it a deliver driver mode of an atom/molecule...whereby its passengers is the "extra" proton/electron.**

**MAGNETISM - best way I can explain this is that... it's like two conjoined twins. They are related, but not the same person. One "happens" only with the other one by its side. When electricity MOVES, or flows, magnetism is produced as well.

MELANIN - skin pigment (color). the tan we get when we are out in the sun is essentially our skin producing more melanin that sits in the skin.

MELANOCYTES - **"melanin producing cells"**

MELANOGENESIS - the process where, ultraviolet light (UV) from the sun, stimluates melanocytes to produce melanin and voila, we have darker skin (a tan)**

**MITOCHONDRIA - the "organ" -elle of the cells of our body where basically the money, or energy is made. It's basically the last step in the processing of our food, water, photons, electrons, that produces the juice to survive and thrive.**

**MOLECULE: unlike an atom, a molecule, is multiple atoms joined together ,attracted, attached, by some type of bond/connection. This creates a structure, a lattice ,a "molecular structure"**

**MYOGLOBIN - KIND OF LIKE HEME + GLOBIN, ONLY FOUND IN MUSCLE TISSUE. ALSO ITS GLOBIN STRUCTURE IS I BELIEVE SLIGHTLY DIFFERENT AND IS OPTIMIZED MORE FOR MUSCLES, HENCE HOW ITS EVOLVED TO BE IN MUSCLES.**

**OXIDATION - on a quantum level, this means the "loss" an electron" Two atoms or molecules collide, react, oxidation occurs to the molecule in question when it loses an electron to another party.. The significance is that on the quantum level electrons are the electricity to our circuits...we are like biological electronics. And this when we are in "oxidative stress" we are in a state of electron deficiency, whereby our "batteries are dyinng. Battery dies, life, cells die. This process, while coined to be related to oxygen (oxygen is like a electron vampire, it attracts it, steals it, and "oxidizes" other parties. However, it doesn't always have to involve oxygen as the electron vampire. It just happens to use "OX" in its name. OXIDATION = "rusting' of our cells...aging of our cells.**

**PHOTON - A "QUANTA" OR UNIT OF LIGHT**

**PORPHYRIN - molecular arrangement whereby an iron atom is at the center, surrounded by a particular arrangement of rings of atoms**

**PROTEIN - a molecular arrangement of atoms such that, its main evolutionary purpose has been to help build the body, repair the body, etc.**

**PROTON: subatomic particle that is intrinsically positively charged. In other words, an arbitrary? "charge" that means it is attracted to an electron, also arbitrary charge. **BASICALLY electrons and protons are two pieces of physical matter that happen to be the ying and the yang, opposites that "attract."**

REDUCTION - to "gain electrons". As electrons are what makes our biological circuits function at a quantum level, reduction is important to be alive. REDOX potential, then becomes how much ability does your body have, how much battery juice (electrons) do you have. More battery life, more abilities you have to combat its "loss" or oxidation" or "aging/rusting/corrosion of our bodies cells.

QUANTUM: as relates to the most reduced, simplified physical piece of matter. A quantum then, is a reduced "piece of the puzzle." A "quantum" of light, a "quantum" of matter = neutron, proton, electron. **EG. the alphabet would be considered a quanta of language. Its a unit of undstanding that holds meaning. We cant reduce our ABC's down any further before it loses meaning.**

QUANTUM ENTANGLEMENT - an unexplainable occurrence (ie a phenomenon) whereby particles, interact with other particles (or in a macro sense ,people, whom are made of particles, interact with one another) and a higher connected energy state seems to result...like a connected, PHYSICAL, new beast. Almost like people physically becoming conjoined, but not at such a level where it's permanent, or visible to the point of an actual conjoined twin. I suppose this could be related on a psychological level, where there seems to be a collective consciousness, where people in different parts of the world feel the same dreams. Quantum entanglement is cool because its like collective consciousness at a physical, concrete, tangible level.

QUANTUM MECHANICS: the mechanism, the interaction, between quanta. Quanta being the most simplified reduced unit, package, of energy that has meaning. EG. the alphabet would be considered a quanta of language. Its a unit of undstanding that holds meaning. We cant reduce our ABC's down any further before it loses meaning.

SUBATOMIC - smaller than an atom. a component of an atom. An electron, proton,or neutron

TOPOLOGY - TOP --- OLOGY. OLOGY = the study of. TOP = best way I can explain it, is it is to do with visual representations of stuff. You got some numbers on excel, and you graph it into a pie chart make 3d data points? That's topology. Topology is physical, it's visual. And in our context of quantum topology, it's about visual representation of quanta (the smallest minute yet significant forms of stuff, packets of stuff, energy, light, etc) in a excel visual..and that visual is on the level of the the quanta moving through space, through time. Waves. The amplitude. The frequency. It's an "angle" of understanding stuff.**

Email late last night from my best friend in Austin, Texas. Her son took his own life on Thursday
evening. My heart cries for her. He was a sensitive and caring young man who had been fully
AWAKE and AWARE of what's going on in this world long before I ever was. But doubt he knew
anything about blue light or grounding or avoiding EMF's. He worked in a big hospital under horrid
lighting all day. That alone is enough to make anyone despondent. In the past 2 years I've been impacted
by the suicide of 4 people and before that, NOTHING. Could this be 5G or just the crazy blue lit world we live in?
Tell your loved ones to get grounded, get sunshine, block blue light and avoid EMF's. I think this tragic epidemic
of suicides is only going to get worse. Perhaps it's all part of the horrid and diabolical plan for humanity. DON'T LET
THE DEMONS WIN - FIGHT THE GOOD FIGHT !

Timing, specifically circadian timing is the key lynchpin to influence brain circuits that control mood and mental processing. When will they realize we can see these quantum effects just by looking at the pupillary response and the way the extraocular eye muscles or facial muscles move. We can even see it with the change in the temperature of your nose. That is how fidelity can be assessed. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29251065](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29251065)

What might the cause of depression really be? Here is today's deep nugget. The proton and neutron making up deuterium can be dissociated through neutral current interactions with neutrinos. The cross section for this interaction is comparatively large, and deuterium was successfully used as a neutrino target in the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory experiment. I have a sense this is used in mitochondria.

Because of this lack of knowledge of how our mitochondria really work……and no one in medicine even knows what deuterium is and how it links to depression.

# Thread: Hello from Iowa - finally making time to get my skin in the game
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-12T06:00:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265215>

Hello to all, I have been following Jack for over a year but was content to just tag along in the shadows. I finally read the leptin Rx this winter while camping on the beach in Mexico. Now I have decided it's time to get in the game. I have so much to learn and am so excited to dive in...I have already realized that the amount of information is overwhelming at first but I am encouraged knowing that I only have to make a little progress every day.
I am now in good health but by no means optimal. I want to get out in front of the storm that is coming and Jack has inspired me to get started. My current battle is that my wife thinks I am crazy, well that is the boiled down version, but we have been separated since June. It allowed me a lot of opportunity to get away from the cold and dark Iowa winter and get myself healthy again. Now my challenge is reaching her with the positive energy that allows me to feel convinced that we can find reconciliation. Thank you Jack for all of your tireless efforts and for inspiring me to let my black swan be seen.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-12T06:40:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265216>

Welcome.......but your wife is sending you the right signal. Go it alone get well and then watch quantum entanglement really begin.

### caroline — 2019-03-12T07:56:02+0200

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-12T16:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265258>

Thanks Jack, It's great to be here. When I look back 6 months I am making amazing progress. I never thought that living in Iowa that I would learn to enjoy cold but I have become addicted to it, and never imagined I could do that on 50-75 grams of carbs daily.
The quantum entanglement that you and JT talk about in his journal is amazing. Thank you for cutting a path through the jungle ahead of us so that our own journeys can be even more rewarding.
I need to learn more about quantum entanglement before I could even ask a good question, but how/where would you recommend starting the journey?

### caroline — 2019-03-13T00:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265298>

If I may .....quantum entanglement is something that just happens when you are working on your own masterpiece.

Look at Jason ....for some reason he knew he had to go to Mexico to meet Jack and Jeremy ...and now they are entangled forever.

The same thing happened to me ....and lots and lots of others.

You need to be working on the three legged stool - for you. Other things will start falling into place.

I don't think you can force quantum entanglement ...or even go somewhere to find it. One day your intuition will kick in and you will see what you need to do - what is right for you and all the things that have been wrong in your life.

One day you will find so much joy in all the simple things that you have missed.

Mother nature is calling you ......and she needs to be your compass. It really is that simple.

Do what Jack does .....every morning at sunrise he waits for his download......

you are right Dan .....here I am on the forum when I am on my honeymoon! I have been asked to be done in 10 minutes - nicely and with a smile ......

so I am outa here....

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-14T04:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265462>

you guys are great support. I have likely been on this path for some time but today was monumental. Not only was I working on an e mail to my wife explaining how I am letting go and setting her free, but she calls me on the phone ( can't even say when the last time was) wants me to come over the house because water got in on the lower level carpet (haven't been there in over 3 months) . So I get the opportunity to tell her in person, If she believes she needs a divorce I am offering to let it happen. I vowed to keep it out of the courts and settle it peaceably. It may cost me an extra 30 or 40 thousand to give here whatever she needs but I'm not willing to put a price tag on all the exposure to bad energy that an ugly divorce could bring into my world. I told her that I still care and that I don't want her to carry any resentment away from the situation, and told her that I am on a different path and won't be able to take any with me either.

still not seeing how to start my optimal journal? This needs to be in it.

### caroline — 2019-03-14T05:19:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-265470>

go to the top left hand side of this page and click on forums.

Count down 5 categories and you will See the heading ....my optimal journal. Click on it and you will see "post new thread"
Give yours a simple name and then start writing!

### caroline — 2019-03-14T05:23:40+0200

So I get the opportunity to tell her in person, If she believes she needs a divorce I am offering to let it happen. I vowed to keep it out of the courts and settle it peaceably. It may cost me an extra 30 or 40 thousand to give here whatever she needs but I'm not willing to put a price tag on all the exposure to bad energy that an ugly divorce could bring into my world. I told her that I still care and that I don't want her to carry any resentment away from the situation, and told her that I am on a different path and won't be able to take any with me either.
>
> still not seeing how to start my optimal journal? This needs to be in it.

well said Ronald .....who knows what your future - or your wife's holds. Our life is precious and we need to treat every day with respect and gratitude. We have been given an amazing gift ...run with it!

Nothing is promised ...but you do need to give it your best shot.

It will be exciting to see where you are a year from now!

### Ronald Waters — 2019-12-17T03:02:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283687>

San Carlos Sonora mexico.
10 hrs and 10 minutes of sunshine today.
Going to Cusco in a month, so far there are 3 in my group but always room for other black swans.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-12-17T16:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283699>

Thats great! What is your impression of San Carlos? Do you have meters? My wife and I both love the sacred vally and have talked of spending time there. We were both impressed with the transparency and kindness of the people. Seems innocent and naive next to jaded Cancun. safe travels

### Ronald Waters — 2019-12-17T19:52:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283713>

We are off grid camping on the beach here about 2 miles from town so I do get cellular service but it's weak. I have an esi 24 with me but I haven't tried it in town. It's a pretty small population of mostly retired northerns.
It's very calm with good supply of seafood but I agree completely with you that the people in South America are easier to mix with. I am stopping off in Colombia and Panama on the way home from Peru so I will have to try to post my thoughts as I travel.

My challenge is to encounter spiritual enlightenment / quantum entanglement.
The real challenge for me is to be vulnerable to the situation I am in.

How do you find interaction with the locals there???

### drezy — 2019-12-17T20:29:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283717>

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=283713)
>
> How do you find interaction with the locals there???

### Inger — 2019-12-17T20:45:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283719>

awesome Ron, now I wish I were there too!!  I am sitting here in the darkness...lol
But I have some stuff to do though.
Let us know how your traveling goes

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=283713)
>
> My challenge is to encounter spiritual enlightenment / quantum entanglement.
> The real challenge for me is to be vulnerable to the situation I am in.

I guess when one tries to search for that, it want happen....
I guess maybe try to just be? Relaxing, being very present, to whatever happens around you?
and there might come small or big situations in your way, that will help you forward  that is when life happens

### Marko Pollo — 2019-12-18T01:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283739>

High five to camping on the beach! You are a quantum role model. We got into Bogota last night and drove to my mother in laws place about 4 hours north. Lots of new cell towers going up everywhere. I was hoping for a lag with new cell technologies, but am not expecting that now. We knew we needed to find lower population density, but am saddened by the speed of roll out. So happy to feel
the sun ! I also love to entangle with people here and there. The people here seem warm and genuine. I like what you said about being vulnerable. I think that helps others to do same and allows awesome entanglement.
We are here till aftet new year, then to Equador. Ojala, our paths will cross!

### Ronald Waters — 2019-12-19T20:54:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283810>

The fish market

9 pound yellowtail fresh from the sea.

Hey Inger,. I still have to make a conscious effort to be present in the moment and to allow myself to be vulnerable to the situation, but little by little it is coming easier..

Are you working on the book? There's still time to get on the Peru Trip, maybe add another chapter to the book?? I'm calling it the quantum entanglement / spiritual enlightenment tour... I'm so excited

### Ronald Waters — 2019-12-19T21:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-iowa-finally-making-time-to-get-my-skin-in-the-game.23289/post-283813>

I was hoping for a lag with new cell technologies, but am not expecting that now. We knew we needed to find lower population density, but am saddened by the speed of roll out. So happy to feel
> the sun ! I also love to entangle with people here and there. The people here seem warm and genuine. I like what you said about being vulnerable. I think that helps others to do same and allows awesome entanglement.

The people in Colombia are great. and yes, the economy and technology, in the cities, are expanding rapidly but I found the concrete buildings to offer far better protection than USA built housing.. and lots of sunshine.

Keep us posted on your Colombia and Equator experience.

The fear of being vulnerable is far outweighed by the payout in quantum entanglement....

# Thread: Suggestions for new laptop?

### Phosphene — 2019-03-12T18:55:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265269>

I’m in the market for a basic laptop that I can hook to an Ethernet cable in the yard, and not be too upset if it accidentally gets left out in the rain.

Just kidding, kinda, but I definitely don’t want anything fancy. Just sturdy, relatively lightweight, low-flicker. (I’ll add Iris software of course, but anything better in affordable screen technology yet?)

Will probably buy refurbished—have had good luck with that.

### JanSz — 2019-03-12T20:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265275>

### drezy — 2019-03-13T16:17:20+0200

I needed a development system for a project I'm working on. Nothing fancy, but I needed windows to run a specific IDE.

I liked this little portable unit ($168).
[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07L49MY9...colid=TVAIZNJ51A5X&psc=0&ref\_=lv\_ov\_lig\_dp\_it](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07L49MY9H/?coliid=IG5EJDXXHQ3NT&colid=TVAIZNJ51A5X&psc=0&ref_=lv_ov_lig_dp_it)
Throw in a $10 usb->ethernet dongle, the $14 Iris software, and \*boom\* Bob's your uncle (as our UK and AUS member's might say).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265344>

Forgot to mention at that size and price just stash it into a gallon ziplock bag and leave in the rain all you want.

### JanSz — 2019-03-13T16:50:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265349>

> Throw in a $10 usb->ethernet dongle, the $14 Iris software, and \*boom\* Bob's your uncle (as our UK and AUS member's might say).

Nice. Phosphene asked for something basic.

Will one be able to listen to Jack's webinars on that machine?
\-----------------
Will it slow down when one opens 10-30 firefox windows, e-mail, few excel spreadsheets and graphic software? Corel Paintshop 2019 (64 bit) in my case.

.

### drezy — 2019-03-13T17:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265352>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=265349)
>
> Nice. Phosphene asked for something basic.
>
> Will one be able to listen to Jack's webinars on that machine?

Probably, Don't overlook the "loudness equalizer" setting mentioned here:
[https://windowsreport.com/increase-laptop-volume-beyond-100/](https://windowsreport.com/increase-laptop-volume-beyond-100/)

Also that article mentions Boom3d audio software. I haven't tried it yet, but I use something like this on MacOS.

> Will it slow down when one opens 10-30 firefox windows, e-mail, few excel spreadsheets and graphic software? Corel Paintshop 2019 (64 bit) in my case.

i'm not sure it could handle your use cases. Note it has 4GB of RAM

You can check your current memory usage like this:
[https://www.wikihow.com/Check-Memory-Usage](https://www.wikihow.com/Check-Memory-Usage)

Also as-is it's in Windows-S mode:
"Windows 10 in S Mode is a more limited, locked-down Windows operating system. In S Mode, you can only install apps from the Store, and you can only browse the web with Microsoft Edge."

It's upgradeable for some money, but it may not float Jan's boat...

I think it's near the bottom price range(yet upgradable) for anyone who half jokingly mentions the possibility of leaving it in the rain.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-18T20:31:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-265816>

Yes, cost-conscious is a kind euphemism but we are similar there at least. He recommended another Chromebook and I was considering that since Iris has a Chrome extension. But it turns out that newly graduated son got a laptop from his job so mom inherited his four year old Toshiba Satellite (originally $450). Not sure how I feel about it, but will give it a go. It’s heavy and the build quality is suspect, but if it lasted this long that’s a good sign.

One of its “features” is a TruBrite HD Display—oy!

Next step: loading and configuring Iris, and getting a super long outdoor Ethernet cable. C7??

### drezy — 2019-03-19T19:32:06+0200

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=265907)

> While I wouldn’t consider us the best DIYers we’ve kluged together enough projects that this should be doable. Thanks again!

### Optimalbound — 2019-04-07T20:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-267563>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=265825)
>
> I’ll need closer to 400 feet to get to my sunspots in the yard with a little slack. Best place to buy in this quantity?

Be aware that the specifications for the protocols on cat5 and cat6a are such that they only support segment lengths of up to 100m (328 ft), and for 10GBASE-T on cat6 it is even shorter.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category\_5\_cable#Maximum\_cable\_segment\_length](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category_5_cable#Maximum_cable_segment_length)
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category\_6\_cable#Maximum\_length](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category_6_cable#Maximum_length)

### Casey55 — 2020-07-19T01:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-new-laptop.23291/post-291042>

A lot of people have problems when they use their laptops for a long time. The problem is that it get filled with viruses and malware. You can stop this by getting the [best pc optimizer](https://thinkmobiles.com/blog/best-registry-cleaner-tools). I got Auslogics, it is really easy to use and very cheap. You download it and install it and when that is finished it will scan your PC. After the scanning it will get rid of all the malware from your PC. It can keep your old laptop clean which will make it possible to work for longer time. You might keep the old one and never buy the new.

# Thread: Ooligan fish aka hooligan aka eulachon aka candlefish DHA and oils content
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ooligan-fish-aka-hooligan-aka-eulachon-aka-candlefish-dha-and-oils-content.23292/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan2 — 2019-03-12T21:47:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ooligan-fish-aka-hooligan-aka-eulachon-aka-candlefish-dha-and-oils-content.23292/post-265282>

This fish was valued by the native tribes of the Pacific Northwest for the high oil content and was the most popular item of trade by natives in and around the NW and even to the Great Lakes region. Not commonly available to buy nowadays, but maybe someone could start a business canning them? Seems like with the popularity of fish oils and DHA nowadays it could catch on again.

[https://www.westonaprice.org/health...ps/native-americans-of-the-pacific-northwest/](https://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/in-his-footsteps/native-americans-of-the-pacific-northwest/)
[http://www.nativeknowledge.org/db/files/tp213.htm](http://www.nativeknowledge.org/db/files/tp213.htm)

### Marko Pollo — 2019-03-13T03:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ooligan-fish-aka-hooligan-aka-eulachon-aka-candlefish-dha-and-oils-content.23292/post-265312>

I was on a fishing boat in SE AK in the 80's one night drinking with about 8 other guys and someone broke out a mason jar of Hooligan grease. The locals treated it like the greatest treat in the world, with everyone dipping thier fingers and eating it plain. I was kind of small minded then and didnt try any. Keep your eyes out for smelt. makes it to the stores seasonally

### drezy — 2019-03-13T16:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ooligan-fish-aka-hooligan-aka-eulachon-aka-candlefish-dha-and-oils-content.23292/post-265345>

[@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/)
Might want to double check. It may have been changed to the less pejorative "behaviorally challenged" fish.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Mmmm. They look yummy. I'd be very temped to start up my smoker if a caught these.

### Dan2 — 2019-03-27T00:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ooligan-fish-aka-hooligan-aka-eulachon-aka-candlefish-dha-and-oils-content.23292/post-266532>

Someone on here start a hooligan business asap

[https://www.mcgill.ca/cine/files/cine/JFoodCompAna1996\_9\_18-31.pdf](https://www.mcgill.ca/cine/files/cine/JFoodCompAna1996_9_18-31.pdf)
(page 6 has DHA ("22:6" in the 2nd chart) content measurements)
"The high levels of DHA (approximately 24% of the total fatty
acids identified) in ooligan grease samples suggests that it is an excellent dietary source of DHA or v-3 PUFA. ... Our results suggest that a daily intake of 20 g of ooligan grease containing about 3.5 g of DHA and 0.1 g of EPA will adequately supply the minimum quantity of v-3 fatty acids (2.4 to 3.6 g per day), noted by Nikkila (1991) to reduce blood cholesterol and triglycerides in hyperlipidemic patients and improve general cardiovascular health.
The reason for the relatively high composition of DHA in ooligan grease compared to ooligan fish extract is not known, but may be attributed to microbial conversion of other fatty acids to DHA during the ripening process. Lipase producing microbes were reported in several lipid modification processes, such as interesterification and acidolysis of fat (Okumura et al., 1976; Kalo, 1986; Osada and Hatano, 1992; Osada et al., 1992; Pabai et al., 1994). This interesting phenomenon awaits further study."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18853216](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18853216)
"...we found EPA and DHA in fresh fish total lipids to be 0.9 and 2.2 wt%, respectively, while in the extracted grease, both were reduced to 0.5 wt%. Only the fresh fish PL fraction contained appreciable DHA. The bulk of the grease consisted of saturated fatty acids (30.3 wt%) and mono-unsaturates (55.0 wt%), explaining its high melting point. Excepting its very low omega-6 content (<2 wt%), oolichan grease is quite similar in composition to human adipose TG."

[https://www.afsc.noaa.gov/publications/procrpt/pr 2006-12.pdf](https://www.afsc.noaa.gov/publications/procrpt/pr%202006-12.pdf)
(243 pages, table of contents pages 9 and 10)

[http://www.bcfoodhistory.ca/ooligan-grease-by-gale-smith/](http://www.bcfoodhistory.ca/ooligan-grease-by-gale-smith/)
