# Thread: Searching the UK coast for great seafood destinations?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CarolandNick — 2019-03-17T10:23:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-265680>

Our trip to the Suffolk coast last weekend has inspired me to start this thread....
We were in the South East collecting the second hand magnetico we found on line and wanted some sea air to make the day complete
A look at the map showed the small seaside town of Aldeburgh was around 40 mins away. What a gem!
The best part for us was the row of traditional shacks on the beach selling fresh caught local seafood. We had a shellfish feast! Simply cooked cockles, langoustines and brown shrimp, but so memorable.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In the North West, where we live, finding somewhere like this is a rare thing indeed. These traditional places have all but vanished.
I would love to know where to visit when I have time off for the great combination of beach, sea and seafood.

To all the UK members - post your links to great places to visit around the coast of our tiny island where you have discovered fantastic seafood - so we can make a point of visiting and feasting!

### CarolandNick — 2019-03-17T10:27:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-265681>

I have to post about this place, too.

This is on the Isle of Skye, at Carbost and well worth a visit if you are in the North.

### mCroon DIablo — 2019-04-11T15:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-267863>

A lovely trip you had there. Not sure if this is something of a place I want to suggest but more of a service that has to do with seafood. I mean I can tell that you like to try new things so I figured I may put my thoughts in to place here. So in the category of seafood, I really enjoy caviar. It's one of my best dishes. Black Caviar to be specific. I've tried many in my time but one I will not yet forget. You can look into [https://osetrus.com/page/faq](https://osetrus.com/page/faq) for more answered questions. I personally loved it for the fact that the caviar is made into the classical malossol, a method historically reserved for the best-grade caviar. It's just a suggestion, thought I might share it with you

### LisaLearning — 2019-04-17T15:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-268284>

Southwold also lovely over that way and lots and lots of seafood - also Mersea Island, although that is south of Colchester.

### CarolandNick — 2019-06-03T10:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-271878>

Black Caviar to be specific. I've tried many in my time but one I will not yet forget. You can look into [https://osetrus.com/page/faq](https://osetrus.com/page/faq) for more answered questions. I personally loved it for the fact that the caviar is made into the classical malossol, a method historically reserved for the best-grade caviar. It's just a suggestion, thought I might share it with you.

mCroon Diablo - this tip is really interesting. Thank you for adding it here. Whenever I have looked at caviar in the UK I have only found stuff that is poor quality and has additives in it. Consequently, it is something I haven't really tried. I have to say that it is a bit out of range for me to buy often, but I will have to get some of this to try. I know it has a really great nutritional profile from my wapf days. I was vegetarian for the first 20 years of my adult life and so I missed out on trying many things. Your experience is appreciated

Thankyou Carol and Nick. Where are you located? I think you have a shop?

What is the name of your friends oyster bar in Dublin? We will be in Dublin on Sept 3rd and 4th and then getting a car and touring around until the 10th. We will definitely need oysters after that long flight!

### CarolandNick — 2019-08-17T12:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-276153>

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - it's taken me ages to get back to you..... the perils of being a shopkeeper, maybe ....
Our shop is based in a small town called Alsager, about 30 miles south of Manchester, but very close to the main north/south road - the M6 (junction 16, then 5 minute drive into town). If you are coming past it would be really cool to meet you. There are so few people around here who have any idea about what we are doing with light, water and magnetism, and although we talk to some of our customers about it, only a small portion are taking any steps to do their own research. It can be a lonely road.
My connection to the oyster bar in Dublin is quite loose - I have met the guy a couple of times at WAPF events., he is lovely - really friendly  He runs an oyster business that makes a dried oyster supplement that we sell. He is called Stephen Kavanagh and a part of his business is running an Irish oyster bar in Dublin's Temple Bar Farmer's Market, Meeting House Square, Temple Bar, Dublin 2, that happens every Saturday.
As for travelling in Scotland - we love it! The Highlands, especially the West Coast and the islands off the coast are so beautiful. It is a place that makes the heart sing when the weather is good! To get hold of good seafood when you are traveling though can be a case of asking the locals because it can be a bit hidden - so much is just sold straight to the world market, rather than being available locally.
We have travelled less in Ireland, but you can be assured of loads of bars with live music, it is a part of the Irish soul
You must be very excited. Have a wonderful trip!

### 8Phoenix — 2019-08-17T14:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-the-uk-coast-for-great-seafood-destinations.23320/post-276156>

# Thread: When you urinate too much what does it really imply about your choices around light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-urinate-too-much-what-does-it-really-imply-about-your-choices-around-light.23324/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-17T14:55:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-urinate-too-much-what-does-it-really-imply-about-your-choices-around-light.23324/post-265700>

More brilliance from Jason the mitochondriac on how the water our mitochondria make from sunlight and respiration lead to Optimal or destruction based upon our choice of how we use the SPECTRUM OF LIFE POORLY OR WISELY. How you choose determines your path.
Environment trumps age as an epigenetic driver. This is true for shift work, ALAN or for a TBI from a car wreck.
Frequent shift work results in more urination for ANY AGE because the circadian gene Rev-erbα controls the production of Cx43, which determines how much urine a bladder holds.
TL;DR: Poor circadian rhythm = More urination = more disease = FASTER DEATH COMES = THE RELATIVITY OF TIME MODELED.
[https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-03-younger-nightshift-workers-shown-pee.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-03-younger-nightshift-workers-shown-pee.html)

THIS IS WHY DIABETICS and people with concussions URINATE SO FREQUENTLY at the wrong times of the day. It shows you that their alpha rhythms on EEG and their SCN control arms are not functioning properly.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-17T14:57:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-urinate-too-much-what-does-it-really-imply-about-your-choices-around-light.23324/post-265702>

Never stop just because you feel defeated. The journey to the other side is attainable only after great suffering and you realizing you have to change your game plan when it FAILS you.

### LieselK — 2019-03-17T15:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-urinate-too-much-what-does-it-really-imply-about-your-choices-around-light.23324/post-265707>

Fascinating. I've noticed that I urinate way less now and have always marveled at my sons' abilities to hold it for so long. I assume we can apply this to kids too? I know kiddos who pee all the freaking time and it's just assumed it's because their bladders are small or because they sip water often. Hmmm... makes you think...

### LieselK — 2019-03-17T15:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-urinate-too-much-what-does-it-really-imply-about-your-choices-around-light.23324/post-265710>

Hmmm, I am guilty of this and don't really see it as a good thing. I see many clients every day and can't stop myself from sharing what I am learning. Many of them are beginning to do some of the easy first steps toward improving their lives. In some ways I feel like a fraud because I am so enthusiastic about these ideas and changes yet I am not my best student. What is that crappy expression? "Those who can, do; those who can't teach." I now choose to believe that I am teaching as I learn and can't be expected to always be the "A" student.

I love all the quotes! Thanks everyone for sharing!!

### Inger — 2019-10-06T19:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krusian-quotes.23325/post-279703>

My vaccine for rejection is Nature.

What gives you solace? After years in utter darkness littered by artificial light, I've forced my eyes into the light of the sun. As long as there is a sunrise in the world, how bad could things be really? I believe Nature is the solace for my soul. This solace place isn't a hiding place. It is a finding place for me. It is a place where I can create who I want to be. In essence, with reflection, I now see Nature has become my anchor, my solace, my opiate of choice. She has become my port of call.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-10-08T16:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krusian-quotes.23325/post-279839>

Apathy. This is being comfortably numb. That is the worst of the worst reactions you can have. ANYTHING else is better. ANYTHING else. Because you can't cook food without a fire...you cannot learn anything about yourself without emotion.

And so... even when someone appears to be eliciting a negative reaction within you, however condescending, arrogant, it may seem... turn that fucking shit into sugar.

And so... this is why Nature is so monumentally good for you, and why tech is so destructive.. because its the difference between being numb and being alive.

# Thread: What would you say when your ex-Lover Is dying a foolish death of their own choosing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-you-say-when-your-ex-lover-is-dying-a-foolish-death-of-their-own-choosing.23326/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-17T15:35:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-you-say-when-your-ex-lover-is-dying-a-foolish-death-of-their-own-choosing.23326/post-265713>

From the files of things that make you go hum.............

**[What would you say when your Ex-Lover Is D](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r5Or6-HOveg)ying a foolish death of their own choosing?**

**There's one thing I want to say so I'll be brave. You were what I wanted then
I gave what I gave. I used to breathe out so you could breathe in
I'm not sorry I met you, but I'm also not sorry it's over.
No, I'm not sorry, because there's nothing to say on their chosen stairway to heaven.

Put some windex on your eyes of disarray to once again see your path.

****When you hit rock bottom, the only way to go is up.****

****Hitting a brick wall gives you the chance to realign your focus.****

****Even the slightest improvement in your life gives you the confidence to carry on.****

****You learn things to prevent mistakes in the future.****

****Your “should do” becomes “must do.”****

****You pursue your goal and work hard even when not rewarded.****

****When you overcome the challenge, nothing seems too big to handle.****

**

# Thread: How much of a prophet Thomas Edison was................????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-of-a-prophet-thomas-edison-was.23327/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-17T22:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-of-a-prophet-thomas-edison-was.23327/post-265737>

Every carb hating food guru needs to know a bit of history.

Thomas Edison, Mr. incandescent light bulb, thought he could outsmart biology, and not surprisingly, had type 2 diabetes as he lived. **Now for his gem of a quote**... “In the old days, man went up and down with the sun… A million years from now, he won’t go to bed at all. _Really, sleep is an absurdity, a bad habit_. We can’t suddenly throw off the thralldom of the habit, but we can still throw it off. Nothing in this world is more dangerous to the efficiency of humanity than too much sleep.”

And we call this idiot a hero of science?

My tribe has to know the disdain I have for him and Tesla. Tesla gave is the AC power grid and millions of chance to screw our biology with light and electricity and we put these two clowns on a pedestal. Tesla died completely mad with mental illness. Two low dopamine cats from their inventions. Now we have billions of lemmings.

Not here we won't.

How to heal your gash? The discontent and frustration that you feel is entirely your own creation. Before you can win, you have to believe you are worthy. The difference between being mediocre and achieving excellence is your thinking to improve your choices. It is on YOU! Run your own race with respect to the spectrum of light you choose to abuse.

I'll stick with the sun thank you.

# Thread: Kate's Journal (from Blue Mountains)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katejc — 2019-03-18T04:45:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-265762>

Hello. I became a member a month ago and this is my first post. I live in the Blue Mountains at the western edge of Sydney. I’ve been following Jack’s work for some time after meeting one of the Founders of EMF Warriors about 18 months ago. Reading some of Jack's blogs has helped me get a better sense of what he is doing and where he is coming from. Great to be here.

I was jaundiced at birth and was not breast fed at all and I have had life-long gut issues (not dreadful as a child but something I have always had to manage with a tendency to constipation which has worsened as I’ve got older; I managed this issue previously by having a high fibre diet and regular exercise.

KEY GENETIC MARKETS

Miotchondrial haplotype – H39

Methylation genes – homozygous for a1298c and clear for the other variant

VDR – homozygous for the BSM variant but clear on the other main variant

CBS – I’m having difficulty working this detail out but found I have CBS rs5742905 (AA) variant.

I am 47 years of age and became rapidly sick when I was 33 in 2005 – with CFS which after 7 years became CFS with EHS (as neurological symptoms developed at that time). The EHS symptoms were tingling hands, feet and head and increasingly regular migraines, which I had experienced only twice before when my periods started, overall muscle weakness (but worse on one side), and balance disturbance. When I first got sick I probably would have said that I was very tired – as my sleep became very disturbed – and that I woke up every morning nauseas, and that the nausea became severe around my periods. Prior to this I had barely any pre-menstrual symptoms at all.

Later when networks got stronger I experienced dizziness at times – a burst when I went onto public transport or into my workplace (after being at home with low RF EMR levels) – which settled down quickly. But other symptoms ramped up – nausea, upset stomach/ gut, headaches, light (man-made) sensitivity, general malaise.

I had a couple of vaccines before flying to Argentina from Sydney (over the south pole) in 2005 – one for yellow fever and one for rabies – and that is when I noticed fatigue initially, so I just took things easily but the fatigue didn’t lift. After I returned from South America I moved into an apartment in the city of Sydney with very high ambient levels of radio frequency EMR (which I didn’t know until years later). I started getting respiratory infections very regularly – say every 6 weeks when I had got a cold once a year perhaps previously – and my sleep deteriorated. Over a period of 6 months I started waking once a night, then twice a night and so on until I woke up 5 times every night and got up in the morning feeling exhausted – and nauseas. The nausea was just awful. I had never had sleep problems before.

Prior to the vaccines I had noticed my gut which had always been sensitive was getting worse ie constipation was getting harder to manage – and my periods changed (from a 4-day cycle to a 2-day cycle).

Fast forward to 2012 – a good friend from university was researching biological effects of RF EMR and came to measure levels in my apartment. I bought a meter, tried to shield (but didn’t own the apartment), so in the end moved. My sleep normalised straight away and the migraines stopped, and the fatigue and muscle weakness also improved over the 1 month I had off work at that time. When I returned to work and the most of the symptoms returned (but not the migraines).

I currently work from home 100% of the time as I am unable to access the workplace without become unwell very quickly – but this workplace is 10m from a large mobile phone base station and his very strong wi-fi.

Key lifestyle changes I have made – all over the last 18 months aside from the move from the city which happened in late 2013:

1.  Moving from an apartment in the city to a house in a low density area with significantly reduced RF EMR levels; no Smart meter on my home at this point in time.

2.  I work primarily from home (wired internet)

3.  I control blue light exposures during the day and night – blue light blocking glasses, IRIS on my computer, and minimise man-made light at night (eg: reading by candle-light).

4.  I don’t wear sunglasses anymore.

5.  I get up and greet the sunrise every morning and ground and get sunlight during the day (as I work from home).

6.  I have Fitzpatrick Type 1 skin-type but have worked on my solar callus and now tolerate sun much better without burning

7.  I follow a seasonal diet – more carbs in late Spring, Summer and early Autumn – but am cutting back carbs now to focus on ketosis over winter. Ketogenic (or almost) really seems to work for me.

8.  I follow the Epi-Paleo diet – ½ dozen oysters twice a week with some prawns and/ or fish, and regular sardines – sometimes cooked; sometimes marinated (so partially raw). I eat arame seaweed on a regular basis now. I make my own organic chicken liver pate so I get some organ meats.

9.  I have had cold baths for the last 4 months or even more but can’t really get the temperature to 10-12 degrees C (50-55% F) – summers are very hot in Western Sydney/ lower Blue Mountains.

10.  I used filtered water – the filter removes 97% of fluoride from tap-water.

11.  DDW when I can afford it ie: 2-months’ worth in Winter last year and 6-weeks’ worth this Summer (from MitoHQ) – a 1:1 dilution with filtered water.

12.  I make regular fish bone broths (using snapper – a wild fish) with a little kelp added when I’m cooking it aside from the usual vegetables, and use Celtic sea-salt or a regular salt high in potassium as my usual salts.

13.  As I got better I also did a little intermittent fasting this Summer – when I could get a good dose of sunlight and ground at the same time.

ONGOING HEALTH ISSUES & KEY BLOODS

I have chronic constipation despite what I do. I usually serve my fish bone broth with ginger juice (made using a Japanese grater) and miso – this helps a bit.

When I had the first batch of DDW in July last year this made the constipation worse – but I slept like a complete log and dreamt a lot – and would wake up completely parched (like I’d walked through a desert). The second batch of DDW did not affect me as much – slightly exacerbated constipation, I didn’t dream as much or feel parched when I woke up, I was slowly getting more energy.

My BUN/CREATININE ratio is not good despite having left city the at the end of 2013 and having worked primarily from home for the last 18 months – and doing all of the above. My last blood test showed a BUN/Creatinine = 18.15 (January 2019).

Vit D levels – in April last year (mid-Autumn) I had a Vit D test = 90ng/ml. A month later at the end of Autumn my EHS symptoms increased dramatically (despite working from home etc) so I went to my doctor and she gave me a slow release Vit D3 shot (to last about 1 year). This immediately reduced my EHS symptoms but I won’t do it again as I’ve read more of Jack’s work. I am considering taking Vit D3 + K2 tablets over winter here (due to the VDR variant).

Vitamin K2 – I read some of Jack’s work on osteopenia/ osteoporosis (as my Dad has that) and had thought perhaps I was deficient in K2 due to long-term gut dysbiosis. I started eating quite a bit of Japanese natto a few weeks ago – as I Iove it – and this caused a skin rash last time I spent quite a bit of time in the sun (with some suncream on) – it looks like polymorphic light eruptions. This has never happened before – and I have been in the sun a lot this summer - and wondered if it was something to the Vit K2??? It has been excruciatingly itchy and appears to be settling down now (very slowly) 2 weeks since it first appeared, but my skin is is still very itchy (and a bit sore) and my muscles are quite sore too. And while the rash started in the most sun exposed areas, other areas have flared up too. Yikes!

My Vitamin D levels were 120ng/ml in January – lots of sun since October last year and through our Summer – but also the Vit D injection.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-20T13:13:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-265984>

It is the springtime of my loving on my tribe.
The second season I am to know in my life
My tribe are the sunlight in my current growing
So little warmth I've felt before as "old Uncle Jack"
It isn't hard to feel me glowing when you read the passion dripping in my words these days.
I watched my fire that grew so low.........I decided to reject living to survive for the ability to thrive.
Technology is killing us. I could care less if you believe it. I know it and so does my tribe. What are the implications of this new idea?
I've been deeply inspired by my desires to change this March. This was reflected in my March 2019 webinar. Here is a new blog to further those thoughts. I want to show you it is possible to dance above the surface of the world. I want to show you it is OK to let your thoughts lift you into creativity that is not hampered by anyone's opinion of you or your life. Divergent thinking is almost always seen as a gift rather than an acquired and developed skill. But this is far from the truth: divergent thinking is a distinct form of higher-order thinking that can be taught to all ages of the students of life I call Black Swans. Great 'divergents' have wild and untamed reality. This is how they choose to live.
**I've found as a mitochondriac being daring never goes out of style.**
[https://www.patreon.com/posts/25499656](https://www.patreon.com/posts/25499656)

### Katejc — 2019-03-27T08:50:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-266556>

I wanted to write about some of my successes and things that have struck me as I've learnt more from reading Jack's blogs and publically available posts. While I've only been a member for a short time, I've been utilising Jack's knowledge and suggestions since the end of 2017 after meeting one of Jack's members who suggested I jump onto EMF Warriors website and checked out the interview they did with Jack.

My first step was to get morning sunlight around sunrise and more sunlight during the day. We were going into Summer in Australia and the sun was already quite strong (in terms of UV levels) and as we were in drought at the time we had a lot of sunny days. And at this time, I had negotiated to work primarily from home due to my sensitivity to digital radiation and therefore was not so knocked out by exposures on public transport and in offices that I had previously been unable to control.

So I decided - no matter how fatigued I was (due to CFS and EHS ... and sometimes poor sleep), the very least I could do was drag my sorry arse outside around sunrise and sit in the sun in my camping chair with my shoes off and have a cup of tea - and greet the day. If I felt better and stronger I'd go for a walk.

I noticed straight away that my mood improved. And I have to say, in this extended period of sickness when I was really in survival mode and just putting one foot in front of the other I had concluded that one positive was that I was not prone to depression. So while I never got depression, just getting mornign sun in Summer in Australia really helped my mood (especially in the early days).

Interestingly, as I did this more over that first Summer, my sleep really improved and I got sleepy and really just wanted to sleep. It was actually difficult to get up in the morning so early (with the sunrise around 6am ... and then earlier as we headed to the Summer equinox). I think my body just really needed sleep after so many years of really disturbed sleep when I lived in the city with high ambient levels of RF EMR.

At the same time I started to eat more fish and started eating lots of sardines which we sometimes get fresh but generally they are caught off the coast of
Western Australia and snap frozen on the boat at -22 degrees C (-8 degrees F). It was a little odd. Initially I really noticed that if I ate sardines for lunch - sitting in the sun a bit - the combination really enhanced my mood. I felt really happy. Odd but true. I have a deep and abiding affection for sardines that many don't share.

Interestingly, some of my EHS sensitivites reduced quite a bit. I don't use Wi-Fi etc at home but I still really notice electrical fields etc from using the computer. The tingly I get really dropped this first Summer. I can't tell you what a relief that was - to finally have some nervous system quietness again.

Anyhow, everything was going swimmingly until May the following year as we went into late Autumn in Australia. To be continued ...

### Katejc — 2019-04-27T07:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-269074>

As I think over many things that Jack has written on 'light, water and magnetism' I realised that Jack has given us the keys to the kingdom. And that we're living in strange disconnected from nature times that necessitate us needing these keys. Thank you Jack

### Katejc — 2019-04-27T07:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-269075>

Continuing on from what I wrote on March 27 - before I had learnt about Jack's work my integrative medicine doctor put me on a very low carb diet - no potatoes, no pumpkin, no bread/ rice/ pasta, no sugar, no honey etc. In terms of fruit, I was allowed only 3 low carb berries a day. I kid you not - I thought she was joking when she said this.

When I started this I was super hungry for about 3 weeks - and for someone who is not really a hungry person - I mean eat your stomach starving. I would have a big dinner with meat and vegetables followed by my 3 berries and double cream for desert with some chopped nuts etc. I would be fine - not hungry - jump into bed and my stomach would start to grumble ... and then almost feel like it was eating itself.

Anyhow, after 3 weeks I had a period and this completely stopped - like overnight.

I did this for 4 months to early summer in Australia and then stopped. Primarily as I was completely over eating so much red meat but also as I kept losing weight.

While I didn't know it - I was kind of doing a bit of a leptin reset.

### Katejc — 2019-04-27T08:19:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-269076>

Back to some successes (and surprises) I've had along the way operationalising some of Jack's suggestions.

As I read more I decided to do some cold thermogenesis - just jumping in a cold bath and dropping the temperature over time from 20C/ 68F to 13C/ 55F (and occasionally lower). I've had a cold bath most nights for the past 6 months - generally in the 15 to 18 degree C range (59-68F) and now we're going into the cool season around 12-13 degrees C (54 - 55F) - and I'm in the bath for 10 - 15 minutes.

I was a little uncertain about doing CT - not because it would be hard/ cold - rather than I don't have much fat to lose and I didn't need to lose weight.

To be frank - it almost felt like the cold baths were more impactful for me than the sunlight strangely. I don't know if this is true or not, but perhaps boosting my mitochondrial magnetism is important in a place like Australia and this really made me feel good.

I LOVE cold baths!!! Here is a photograph of me taken in one of the mountains streams in the upper Blue Mountains by a friend. The water temperature is 13C/ 55F and my feet/ legs don't ache at all. I have since jumped into this spot up to my neck at that temperature for 15 minutes.

As I wander about the Blue Mountains a feel like there are Mountains Mermaids who have a siren song calling us to sunny spots at streams to soothe our heartaches and troubles and remind us of our natures.

### Katejc — 2019-04-27T08:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journal-from-blue-mountains.23328/post-269077>

One question someone might be able to answer - regarding CT.

I did CT in a mountain stream recently that was at 12C/ 54F and was in the stream for 15 minutes - the first 10 minutes fully immersed up to my next and the last 5 sitting in a small waterfall. I felt fine but could feel a bit of a shiver internally so got out, dried off and got dressed. This little internal shiver tells me that my body temperature is dropping and I need to get out.

After getting dressing I got some serious shivers but not unmanagable - teeth chattering a bit and hands shaking a bit too. When I got into the car I noticed that I really needed to take some deep breaths for about 5 minutes - almost like I was hyperventilating (as I was very cold). Any thoughts?

### DrEttinger — 2019-03-18T16:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors-reverse-recommendation-on-aspirin.23330/post-265796>

Using a drug, any drug, long term (30+ days), is in most, not all cases, an illogical measure. We're not born with a need for cholesterol, blood thinning, sugar, blood pressure... medication, but MILLIONS of people take them daily. Why? Because millions of people daily take their God-given body for granted and abuse it to the point that it short circuits. Rather than use logic and change behaviors, ignorance is continued and drugs will be added to the mix in an effort to mask their crime. It's a funny world we live in where NATURE is so untrusted and MAN is glorified with his inventions and potions. MAN will never improve upon what nature has already put into place.

[Don't take an aspirin a day to prevent heart attacks and strokes: Doctors reverse recommendation](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/health/2019/03/18/aspirin-prevent-heart-attacks-strokes-doctors/3199831002)

### Cheryl. — 2019-03-19T09:16:04+0200

# Thread: Jack Kruse Podcast Curriculum - Youtube Edition V1

### OliverGruener2 — 2019-03-18T16:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-265798>

My idea was to create a document \[for myself\] that helps me organize some of the content JK produced \[mostly through podcasts as a guest\]. I recently noticed that Jack sometimes has more in depth explanations for the quantum physics etc. I think to some degree it really depends on the host, how good the relationship between the two of them is, how good the questions are and so on.

I had some eureka moments here and there and I want to create a JK Curriculum for myself because I want to understand the science. So every time [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) says "you don't need to understand this..." I say "eh, yeah, I do, I want to...gimme!!" :-D

This is the "Podcast Youtube Edition". I think I covered the most prominent on Youtube but maybe I missed some.

Feel free to add future (or past) YT episode to the list...

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kQCaVGJBoEP\_MtiFWg0dWxPK42qy6mM9Kf1zAahon0k/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kQCaVGJBoEP_MtiFWg0dWxPK42qy6mM9Kf1zAahon0k/edit?usp=sharing)

### mrc — 2019-05-15T13:18:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-270503>

5/8/19
Today we discuss:

-   Why getting outside as much as possible is so important to our health.
-   The positive impact of seeing every sunrise.
-   The role our mitochondria play in weight management and health.
-   A challenge to take control of your own health.

Full interview text is in the link
"...if you're a clinician, if you want to help people, you need to begin to get rid of the own half lies in your own life, and that's what I've been doing for 15 years.
I realized that everything I learned in medical school was really a half truth.
When I started subtracting the half truths and started putting nature's laws at the top of the list, that's when the needle started to move for me, it started to move for my patients, and I'm no longer willing to remain silent about what I've learned."

I disagree with Jack any significant litigation will occur or succeed against 5G. He cites "big" tobacco as precedence but follow the next link which shows they've only been forced to pay $126 billion in the past 20 years. Tobacco settlement was just simple appeasement, more dog and pony show for the masses. [https://publichealthlawcenter.org/sites/default/files/resources/MSA-Overview-2018.pdf](https://publichealthlawcenter.org/sites/default/files/resources/MSA-Overview-2018.pdf)

The real stuff like 5G is "too big too fail." Even something as minor as amalgams would have a liability in the trillions if every American with them had the holistic dental protocol operations used to replace them. But ADA says mercury is not toxic end of story. Maybe they'll set up something like the vaccine court where they'll deny most claims and give out a few billion for more dog and pony. Actually there won't be any claims as there hasn't been with 1 to 4G because nearly all the science has been bought and corrupted.

Also basic types of glass do let in most UVA and some UVB. No way the tropical conservatories I visit would have thriving plant and tree life if glass blocked all the UV.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-06-25T16:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-273255>

Improve your ability to think.

-   -   Thinking is not IQ. When people talk about thinking they make the mistake of thinking that people with high IQs think better. That’s not what I’m talking about. I hate to break it to you but unless you’re trying to get into Mensa, IQ tests don’t matter as much as we think they do. After a certain point, that’s not the type of knowledge or brainpower that makes you better at life, happier, or more successful. It’s a measure sure, but a relatively useless one.

        If you want to outsmart people who are smarter than you, temperament and life-long learning are more important than IQ. Become a life long learner and do it under the power of the sun and the absence of the sun during night. Nothing works better or is as simple for the functioning of the human brain says this brain surgeon.

        Two of the guiding principles that Black Swans should follow on their path towards seeking wisdom are: (1) See the sunrise every AM and go to bed smarter than when you woke up; and (2) I’m not smart enough to figure everything out myself, so I want to ‘master the best of what "parts" of the truth other people have already figured out.’

        Acquiring wisdom is hard work. Learning how to think is tougher. It means sifting through the information, filtering the bull shit, and connecting it to a framework built around Nature's wisdom that you can use. A lot of people want to get their opinions from someone else who is selling a product or narrative. I know this because whenever anyone blurts out an opinion on social media I know is suspect and I ask why, I get some hastily re-phrased sound-byte that doesn’t contextualize the problem, identify the forces at play, demonstrate differences or similarities with previous situations, consider base rates, or … anything else that would demonstrate some level of thinking. One of my favorite questions to probe thinking is to ask what information would cause someone to change their mind. Immediately stop listening and leave if they say ‘I can’t think of anything.’ That is how I operate my circle of six. I want humans who know how to pack their own parachutes because they think well.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-30T14:29:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-343083>

Another new podcast out this holiday weekend:
When we believe we cannot change a situation, we should begin to think about changing ourselves. The real art of living a great life is becoming excellent at managing its transitions.
The true measure of personal leadership is done by the quality of the leaders they leave behind as their legacy, not by the number of followers they garnered. Great leaders are like great conductors who demonstrate their ability to create an environment where the artistry of others may emerge so that their teams can perform excellently!
The conductor is the only person in the orchestra who “doesn’t make a sound." Like musical notes, they have their specific place and timing; their part is to play through the sharps and flats to produce a symphony of excellence to the ear.
Conducting does not demand or control. Conducting well aligns people according to their unique talents and gifts and guides them so their whole lives are in harmony. A well-conducted life perceives, believes, and receives its own dreams while helping others reach their dreams. A well-orchestrated life delivers a high-yield, high-fidelity signal. Today, begin to color your own masterpiece.
Appreciation is a beautiful thing. It makes what is excellent in others belong to us as well. You are part of my story, memories, and scenery. Thank you for being open to nature's messages. Nature conducts me.

### [Dr Jack Kruse - Decentralized Medicine | The Health Sessions - The Best in Health, Wellness & Human Performance](https://thehealthsessions.com.au/episode/dr-jack-kruse-decentralized-medicine)

This week, I’m excited to welcome Dr Jack Kruse. Dr Kruse is a board certified neurosurgeon, health educator, and proponent of unconventional health and...

thehealthsessions.com.au

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-01T16:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-343123>

Here is a new podcast that might be the best podcast I have done in 2024. What do I long for right now? Freedom, liberty, and allodial title. All I want is to lessen the pain I feel every night when I see violent protests tear the fabric of both away.

People have only as much liberty as they have the intelligence to want and the courage to take. Liberty allows for disobedience, but that disobedience needs to be peaceful.

Liberty gives us the ability and the right to tell people what they do not want to hear. This is the basis of leadership.

Freedom is not something that anybody in government can give. "We The People" take freedom, and people are only as free as they choose to be.

But every morning I wake up is another day, hopeless and miserable, with nothing but a deafening silence, a wave of tears, memories, and your absence in my life. That will change in 2025.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-343877>

After 10 hours of recording with Breedlove, what is the lesson being delivered?

DISTILLATION OF WISDOM IS A STORY OF TIME

Do you think you use time wisely?

Do you prioritize your choices by weighing the value of information and wisdom against the cost of choice and decision?

Life is always wrapped in opportunity cost. We had pushback on this topic during the pod.

Decentralized medicine is both science and art in a unique package blended by perspectives of how the world is affected by Nature.

From this vantage point, a good decentralized artist knows he has less time than ideas, and this pushes him to act, create, and share the ideas born in his craft. Time is too precious to waste.

You are the artist who paints the canvas of your life.

The job of Nature is to deepen the mystery for the artist continually. The artist's job is to find the inspiration of Nature and reflect it in your creation and wisdom.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-04T15:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-344712>

Bitcoiner Robert @breedlove\_22 came to visit me in El Salvador and spent four days interviewing me at my home in mid December 2024. Here is the first 4 hours of the 12 hour discussion caught on tape. Keep in mind that new ideas are commonplace in centralized science, and almost always wrong because of the fundamental we believe. You'll hear about this in this podcast when I reference time realtivity and the RCT. They are incongruent, but modern medicine is BASED on this incongruent belief.

Therefore, it should be no surprise to you that most flashes of centralized insight lead nowhere; statistically, they have a half-life of hours or maybe days. Most experiments to follow up the surviving insights are tedious and consume large amounts of time, only to yield negative or (worse!) ambiguous results. Decentralized science is a far more productive endeavor.

Part 3 of the WIM podcast is now live and might the best part of the trilogy.

Our country is run by federal orders from unelected bureaucrats but in 2025 we call it 'following the guidelines' to make people feel better. They are usurping power in unConstitutional ways. Stop complying with their edicts immediately.

The Federal Reserve is in the business of injustice. Inflation is time theft or legalized counterfeiting. An unjust law is a code that is out of harmony w/ the moral law. To put it in the terms of St. Thomas Aquinas: An unjust law is a human law that is not rooted in eternal law and natural decentralized law

The bankers will ensure we stay in debt. The pharmaceutical companies will ensure we stay sick. The church will keep you in fear. The weapons manufacturers will ensure we keep going to war. The media will ensure we are prevented from knowing the truth. The Government will ensure all of this is done legally s they can manitain their control and you remain their economic slave.

The first time you push back against the bully, signals the end of his power over anyone.
Bitcoin is that pushback every time you buy a satoshi.

Nature leads me, so I can lead my tribe back to Nature.

Unlocking a person's potential to improve is creative leadership. Only the divergent mind contemplates changing themselves or their perspective to lead themselves to a better state of being. Success is ultimately a realization that you must value yourself to see a dream become reality. Nothing is more seductive to a divergent mind then unknown regions trapped within.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-27T15:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-345826>

We don’t have to say everything to be a spark or a light for others. Sometimes, a fire built at the beach will attract interested people to your campfire. Folks are guests in our journey, the same way we are guests in theirs. But we all meet each other because every person is a personal lesson waiting to be told. When I meet people this way, they become chapters in my novel of life.

Great leaders never set out to be leaders; they set out to make a difference in the world. It is never about their role; however, it is about their goal.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-04T19:13:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-347869>

This is the best podcast I've done to date. I've been deeply influenced by a group of people I speak about in this podcast. Over the last 35 years, I have been entangled with many powerful people who have inspired me to write about my philosophy for life.

How do you touch others in your own life? Do you use your hands? Your words? Your ideas? Is touch physical for you, or can we do it wirelessly? This podcast reflects this.

Must it be used in close proximity, or can your ability to reach people expand? Can you connect well to anyone anywhere and at any time?

Reading deep thoughts of ideas you get is like undressing before a bath. You don't undress out of fear that your clothes will become wet. You undress because you want the water to touch your body. You want to immerse yourself entirely in the feeling of the water and to emerge anew from the experience.

The most substantial ties we touch others with are the invisible threads nature uses to connect to us.
Touch is a very complex thing that is intoxicating and intangible for most.

Friends might lose touch, but they never seem to lose feelings........Friends tend to misplace their power to touch us. Wounded animals touch each other, and surgeons must touch their patients to heal them. The sun has to touch us to regenerate ourselves to live. Touch is incredibly complex, and how we apply it or misuse it defines our lives.

THE WISE IN LIFE DO NOT FEEL THE TOUCH OF PRESSURE.......THEY APPLY PRESSURE TO TOUCH THINGS THAT MATTER TO THEM.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T20:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-348680>

New podcast out: Nature is the way of seeing the truth. Only Nature penetrates human habit, pride, passion, and intelligence to reveal wisdom. Nature erects a boundary around us that tethers us to her. Sunrise and sunset are two such boundaries. If we stray too far from the laws in either, we pay a deep toll. The relationship between what we see and what we know is never settled. Each morning we see the sunrise. We know that the earth is turning toward the sun to create the picture we see. Yet the knowledge, the explanation, never quite fits the vision we experience and we never really understand how it renews us.

That is due to a lack of awareness/dopamine that creates our need for transformation. This demand is built in by seasonal light changes. The demand is not great at the equator but rises to extremes at higher latitudes. This is why life is sparse above 60 latitudes.

Transformational change is a state of bold total annihilation or what we were to something we envision ourselves to be. It can not simply be a better version of the business-as-usual approach. Transformational learning is a shift in our context of reality and our point of view about ourselves

The raw materials of nature are chaotic, but she uses her recipes simply to find harmony. We must find out about that melody in nature. Everything is linked. There is not even a small part in all nature that is a soloist, for every part is a relative substrate of one thing or force to another to create harmony and coherence of part in itself. This is how the sums of the parts, are greater than the whole = Bitcoiners.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-20T15:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-348951>

This new podcast should be an eye-opener for many.

A little-known warning from the manufacturer is hidden within your cellphone manual. Do you know what it says? What if I told you it says the NTP study of 11/1/18 is a bigger deal than you want to know? It should be no surprise if you pay attention to the literature that has already documented the health effects of RF and microwave radiation... the inverse square law is your friend. Don’t put this electrosmog by your brain if you want to avoid the healthcare system.
Divergent thinking is the center of human creativity, and most people believe we are born with it, but I do not believe this. We can teach people how to think more broadly by learning to examine things we do not believe or accept to see if they have merit or not. Divergent thinking can be acquired and developed as a skill when you get "skin in the game." I now excuse people who sense life with tiny thinking. This brand of expansive thinking is a distinct form of higher-order thinking, and it can be taught to all ages of autodidacts. "Divergents" always give themselves permission to see things differently than crowds. Misunderstanding vanishes when you train yourself to ask, 'What else might the 11/1/18 NTP study on RF radiations imply'? Could it cause neurodegeneration since nnEMF increases AMPk pathways and glucose metabolism?
You better start asking better questions, folks.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-21T15:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-349043>

This new podcast has lots of new ideas.

DECIDE WHAT’S MORE IMPORTANT THAN PEOPLE’S FEELINGS before you agree to engage/treat 1. Most people let feelings dictate their life, both their feelings and those of others. They don’t even realize this because they haven’t stopped to think about it. But if you allow your life to be dictated by emotions, then you’re going to be stuck in a shithole predicament forever. The key to developing the willingness to upset other people is understanding what’s more important than them being upset. Would you hurt someone’s feelings to save a dying family member? Yeah, probably. What about to save your career? I would hope so (some low-dopamine folks don’t). What about promoting a good cause you care about? Unethical assholes are assholes because they care about themselves more than others. They are narcissistic and see the world only in terms of what benefits themselves. That isn’t good. They are unethical because their cause is terrible. Finding a good reason beyond one’s interests is the first step to becoming an ethical asshole. These are the people I seek to collect in my network. If you do not fit the bill, I will cut you on every social media platform I find you on. I want no terrible viral infections ruining my tribe. 2. Know who you want to treat. There should be people with SERIOUS skin in the game. Who are your people, and how do you search for talent? The Black Swan is always looking for diamonds in the rough in places where few consider looking. So, for talent and passion, how do you find the best people in the world to be your team of exceptional people? Similarly, how do great people get noticed by those who are looking for these people? The wise avoid trends. They avoid "group thinking" when looking for a potential decentralized MD.

After reflecting on this podcast, it becomes clear that true wellness isn’t about passively accepting what the centralized structures offer, but about actively reclaiming your health by trusting in the raw truths that nature provides. To be a “mitochondriac” means understanding the power within you—resisting the manipulation of those who profit off your ignorance and settling for nothing less than what you truly deserve: autonomy, clarity, and vitality. You are not a passive participant in your health journey. Embrace your role as an illness martyr, one who stands unwavering against the false narratives, and carve your own path to true well-being. Trust yourself, trust nature, and never let the noise of conventional wisdom drown out your innate truth. Never trust any government to tell you any truth.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-26T04:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-349360>

Nature does not hurry and yet everything is accomplished because of how she uses enegy in decentralized fashion.

Anyone who calls mtDNA’s role “narrower” idea than nDNA is sloppy way of thinking. Mitochondrial DNA isn’t just some sidekick; it’s foundational to human life. Without the energy a body is a cadaver and energy drives oxidative phosphorylation, nDNA’s instructions are dead in the water. I think I am absolutely right: DNA without the consideration of energy is a cadaver—lifeless matter, no direction, no execution. Doug Wallace’s work on mitochondrial genetics and my emphasis on bioenergetics would indeed shred any downplay of mtDNA’s primacy. Wallace has spent decades showing how mtDNA mutations underpin chronic diseases, and I hammer the point that energy flow dictates life itself.
The intent of the paradigm in power in centralized medicine is to dismiss mtDNA’s importance. They do not want you or your doctors to realize its abundance in plasma and insane mutation rate associated with mtDNA screams that it’s a signal worth listening to. The paradigm angle is more about why centralized medicine fixates on nDNA: legacy bias, easier targets, quicker payoffs for BigHarma via the med school curriculums. Big Harma is playing the long game here to treat and cultivates it clients like a beef farmer cultivates his herd. I do not think I am wrong to call out the imbalance of these opinions in this space. If anything, mtDNA’s role in energy production makes it the linchpin, not a footnote in the modern problems in centralized healthcare. Research dollars chasing nDNA while mtDNA’s screaming “I’m the power plant!” does look like a profit-driven blind spot when you zoom out. The scientific bottleneck built arounf complexity of heteroplasmy, lack of tools, is a real issue, but it’s no excuse for sidelining what keeps the whole system humming. ENERGY.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-29T23:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-349602>

New podcast that is fire.

In the crucible of adversity, where pressure mounts and challenges loom large, we uncover the raw essence of accountability and purpose. Today, March 29, 2025, the United States stands at a precipice—its political landscape fractured by unrelenting division, economic uncertainty, and a relentless tug-of-war between ideals. Our burdens, from the erosion of trust in institutions to the fierce debates over liberty and justice, are not mere weights to bear; they are the very forge in which our national character is tempered. When failure threatens—be it through legislative gridlock, cultural discord, or the specter of external threats—we are jolted from complacency, compelled to confront our fears rather than bow to them. Adversity, in its unyielding beauty, strips away illusion and reveals our resilience: a people capable of rising, as we always have, through the storms of democracy. In this moment, our strength lies not in surrender to the obstacles, but in the courage to wield our collective purpose against them.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-10T00:18:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-350388>

New podcast out with Dr. Bronson an internal medicine MD member of mine.

The Universe Is Indifferent: Your Will Isn’t, and Centralized Medicine’s Lies Must End
The MAHA movement’s food-first approach, while well-intentioned, misses the mark because it fails to address the photo bioelectrical nature of life a truth buried by the Rockefeller-driven centralized medical system that turned sickness into a subscription model, hospitals into businesses, and doctors into salesmen, all while banning natural cures like sunlight exposure.

Chronic disease is not a nutritional problem; it’s a light problem. Conditions like pterygium, dry macular degeneration, and NAFLD driven by the Bazan Effect where DHA destruction in the retina leads to systemic lipid dysregulation stem from our failure to align with nature’s signals, starting with sunrise. By realigning with these rhythms, we can restore our mitochondrial health, support the TCA cycle, and mitigate the paramagnetic flip that drives modern illness.

Yet, the most damning indictment of centralized medicine lies in its insidious indoctrination of both patients and doctors, who believe they’re following “science” when, as the slide starkly reveals, they’re actually listening to paid promotional ads by trillion-dollar industries that make up their own science—science that benefits them, not you. This deception runs so deep that even educated MDs think they’re being objective, when nothing could be further from the truth: take non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), where blue light and nnEMF shift heme proteins in the liver to the Fe³⁺ oxidation state, shutting down the TCA cycle and preventing fat burning, leaving fats sequestered in the liver.

Yet Big Pharma pushes drugs for this condition, knowing full well that no MD will ever tell you how light affects iron’s oxidation state, because this fundamental truth was deliberately excluded from medical school curriculums by the very industries that profit from your ignorance. The universe is indifferent; your will isn’t. That’s the wisdom that turns thorns into a throne. Time to decide what you’ll settle for. Mitochondriacs—those who prioritize their mitochondrial health by embracing sunlight and rejecting centralized dogma—never settle for less than they deserve. They understand that nature’s electrical fabric is the key to thriving, not just surviving. So, step into the sunrise, reclaim your health, and build your throne from the thorns of a world that’s forgotten nature’s wisdom, casting aside the lies of a centralized medical system that has betrayed us all. Only then can we truly make America—and the world—healthy again, free from the control of a system that has buried nature’s recipes and led to at least 50 catastrophic errors in modern healthcare. That will be covered soon on my blog.

### Revelation — 2025-04-12T16:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-350514>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=273255)
>
> Improve your ability to think.
>
> -   -   Thinking is not IQ. When people talk about thinking they make the mistake of thinking that people with high IQs think better. That’s not what I’m talking about. I hate to break it to you but unless you’re trying to get into Mensa, IQ tests don’t matter as much as we think they do. After a certain point, that’s not the type of knowledge or brainpower that makes you better at life, happier, or more successful. It’s a measure sure, but a relatively useless one.
>
>         If you want to outsmart people who are smarter than you, temperament and life-long learning are more important than IQ. Become a life long learner and do it under the power of the sun and the absence of the sun during night. Nothing works better or is as simple for the functioning of the human brain says this brain surgeon.
>
>         Two of the guiding principles that Black Swans should follow on their path towards seeking wisdom are: (1) See the sunrise every AM and go to bed smarter than when you woke up; and (2) I’m not smart enough to figure everything out myself, so I want to ‘master the best of what "parts" of the truth other people have already figured out.’
>
>         Acquiring wisdom is hard work. Learning how to think is tougher. It means sifting through the information, filtering the bull shit, and connecting it to a framework built around Nature's wisdom that you can use. A lot of people want to get their opinions from someone else who is selling a product or narrative. I know this because whenever anyone blurts out an opinion on social media I know is suspect and I ask why, I get some hastily re-phrased sound-byte that doesn’t contextualize the problem, identify the forces at play, demonstrate differences or similarities with previous situations, consider base rates, or … anything else that would demonstrate some level of thinking. One of my favorite questions to probe thinking is to ask what information would cause someone to change their mind. Immediately stop listening and leave if they say ‘I can’t think of anything.’ That is how I operate my circle of six. I want humans who know how to pack their own parachutes because they think well.

Pearls of Wisdom... goes back to what Einstein shared, simplicity is where it is at. I think the definition of "it" is truth. Seeking truth is what keeps us in the humility camp, always willing to adjust and change course.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-26T03:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-356986>

New podcast out today. When you understand the GOE well, you begin to see that Oxygen didn't give Earth complex life; rather, oxygen gave complexity a chance by allowing time to pass more slowly. In this way, life is the slowest form of death we create.

The paradox of life as the Slowest Form of death implies that life is the slowest form of death we create, introducing a thermodynamic and existential dimension. Here’s how it expands:

Thermodynamic Perspective: Life defies entropy locally by organizing matter into complex structures, fueled by oxygen-driven energy. However, this process accelerates the universe’s entropy on a grand scale, as energy is dissipated as heat. The GOE’s oxygen surge intensified this cycle, enabling life to delay its own dissolution and death by prolonging its metabolic dance with oxygen. Each breath we take is both a renewal and a step toward entropy, making life a prolonged surrender to the second law of thermodynamics.

Biological Mortality: Oxygen, while sustaining life, generates ROS that damage DNA, proteins, and lipids over time, contributing to aging and eventual death. The GOE’s legacy includes this double-edged sword: it gave complexity a chance by enhancing energy and protection, but embedded a slow decay mechanism. Breath-hold practices (intermittent hypoxia) and UV-A interactions counter this by optimizing ROS management, echoing the GOE’s adaptive strategies to extend the duration of life.

Philosophical Implications: If life is the slowest form of death, it suggests a cosmic irony; our existence is a temporary resistance against time’s erosion, enabled by oxygen’s gift. The GOE didn’t create life but reframed it as a prolonged negotiation with mortality. This aligns with my view of breath as communion, a deliberate pause to reconnect with nature’s design, slowing the drain of our finite time.

Picture the GOE: oxygen surges, mitochondria emerge, and ROS threaten life’s fabric. Nature responds with sleep, a daily near-death where thanatotranscriptomic genes, hemifusomes, and UPE orchestrate a rebirth. We die each dusk, mitochondria purged and repaired, UPE guiding the dance of water and redox. At dawn, we rise, renewed, our lifespan stretched by this oxygen-bought time. NDEs echo this cycle, a glimpse of the edge we navigate nightly. My thesis reveals sleep as nature’s masterpiece, transforming death into a slow, regenerative art, a gift that keeps us complexly alive.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-31T17:43:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-357288>

It is not the things we see that matter most, it is the little things we cannot see, that are covert, that do. They determine the type of life we get and what determines our outcomes is how those little things crash, pass, and enter us to make us alive. Nature is a work of art. Cells let her fabric, waves, and rhythms penetrate us.

This idea won't be popular, but it is true.

It's the unseen, covert elements, like the nanotunnels in mitochondria that leave evidence of a previous electrical scar that signal a change in charge dynamics within our cells, that shape our lives. These microscopic interactions, how they crash, pass, or enter, drive our vitality, as nature's art weaves its fabric, waves, and rhythms through us. This is a poetic nod to the science of life that I tried to get accross in this podcast.

This is a poetic nod to the science of life that I tried to get accross in this podcast.
>

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T00:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-358251>

The podcast is live:

We all paint the picture of our lives with our actions and our perspectives, yet few of us realize we are the artist. What is important in life is life is how we choose to perceive the canvas; being happy doesn't mean that everything is perfect. It means that I have decided to look beyond the imperfections. People with the right perspective see opportunity everywhere, and they see problems as possibilities.

We see the rainbow not just the storm. We see the potential in people. People in life are like gold. We must dig through a lot of dirt in order to experience their magic. When we find gold we must value it; look at the present moment with them as a gift, and stop using the future with them to escape the present. We need to marvel at the gifts we have now and everyday. We see the big picture in life. We realize the more we lift and light up others the more depth of field our painting takes on. The more we do this the more people are drawn to see how "we paint" our canvas.

We are here to value life and the "right perspective" will often change everything.......a narrow perspective limits your vision of what is possible. It puts your mind in a box that is hard to escape. In life, we are always in the middle of someone's perspective, so we have to always stay in the shadow of our eternal promises, so we that we are at the head and not the foot of their perspectives.

This is where the magic happens with others. As long as our perspective deals with what is beneficial and uplifting for others, there's really no problem at all. We can live in harmony with nature and with ourselves. Make these moments important, vital, and worth living.

I listened to this podcast in full and thought Martin Picard carried the interviewer. Martin's statement when he got to the edge of his own scientific knowledge about what health is in terms of energy, atomic structure, and information was the key takeaway for the Savages. The interviewer was way out of his league, and he detracted from Martin's ideas. Martin's discussion with the audience was on Morowitz's theorem of energy flow. Gold members and higher gain access to archived Webinars (mp4 or mp3 files).

At JackKruse.com (the free, non-member part of the site), the first blog post I could locate is called, "[Living An Optimized Life: The Dr. Jack Kruse Story](https://jackkruse.com/optimized-life-cenegenics-weight-loss-bioidentical-hormone-replacement-growth-hormone/)".

What better way to consume the information than using the order provided by the author/innovator himself? (I also recommend to first read all 30 levees of The Quilt several times to get the macro context. Print them out and keep handy as you read each blog. See how the quilt pieces fit.

Use the "previous" and "next" buttons to chronologically go through the content. Each blog post is dated, located at the end of the post. Along the way are Webinars, which are available to Gold members and higher. I suggest upgrading to gold to access the content, read along and/or listen to the monthly webinars.

Hi rtd
I am a gold member, since May 2025 and when time allows here I listen to previous webinars and have attended most of the PowWows since May. Unfortunately, this status has yet to be reflected in the forum space. I have contacted [@Support](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1/) about this as well, to no avail.

I am in the final weeks of living full time in my home country, making money and preparing the last of my belongings to be stored indefinitely and your suggestions are right in line with what I plan to bring with me to ES. I will print all of the documents I can find, bring along a few binders and study materials to help but I learn better from the addition of auditory processing, hence the question about an audible version of the material. This, understandably, would be a monumental undertaking but maybe it could become Dr Kruse’s own podcast in future. No guests, just him narrating his material.

Johan’s suggestion is also good but I really love listening to Dr Kruse explain his concepts and material over having it read by AI or software. I’ve listened to so many podcasts, I end up revisiting them and each time I do, I learn more and more from them. Regardless, my trip will be one for studying. Both Dr Kruse’s work and a deep dive into the Bible as well. (Maybe learn some Spanish too!)

Thank you both for the thoughtful responses.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-20T22:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-podcast-curriculum-youtube-edition-v1.23331/post-359770>

NEW PODCAST OUT: People have asked me what do I make about the current events that happened in Utah last week. Well here was my take on it right after it happened.

The United States government is unthinkably powerful in too many aspects of our lives.

But the geniuses who created America via our founding documents understood the risks of allowing a single government entity to have too much power. They intended strong states as counterweights to the national government.

The 10th Amendment makes this restraint explicit: The powers not delegated to the United States by the Constitution, nor prohibited by it to the States, are reserved to the States respectively, or to the people. Over time, though, the federal government has taken power at the expense of the states. Some of this shift was inevitable, a reaction to the growth in powerful multinational companies and wars that raged globally, hot and cold.

Still, the Constitution and its limits remain.
I believe those limits will prove particularly useful during the Bitcoin transition from the fiat system.

UNCLE JACK’s THEOREM
Proof-of-stake = Trust in people
Proof-of-work = Trust in the mathematics of Nature
Nature = Proof of work = quantum mechanics = probabilities

Bitcoin is a truth machine that produces a time chain that builds an orange brick road to freedom for taxpayers. There is no rule that says politicians cannot give it to the people to use.

Bonds are the side chain of fiat that becomes less like money under the force of chronic inflation. Bitcoin ends the need to participate in this side chain. Examine your fiat past, take from it, and leave it there. The real work in life's currents is to break the rules we rigidly establish for ourselves.

I am 100% fully aware. One of the few. It’s literally impossible to find “entertainment“ that isn’t laced with the programming. Even if the acting, directing, effects, etc are phenomenal,it’s difficult to be entertained because it’s infuriating. Even as a child I knew something was going on with the TV. It didn’t add up. I tried quizzing my mom about it, but I just didn’t know how to articulate what I was picking up on. Moving to Boulder was the best thing I could have done in 1996. I met a lot of hippies who didn’t watch TV, who didn’t live in the “matrix”. Boulder was very different back then. I was inspired to unplug the TV for 30 days to see what it would be like. I had to twist my husbands arm but he agreed. After 3 days “the fog” lifted. I had a clarity about the world that I had been unable to achieve while being brainwashed by the idiot box. We didn’t turn it back on until the week before 9/11 when we bought a new TV to be able to watch VHS movies occasionally. We had cable internet at the time and plugged it into the back of the TV and got basic cable with no cable box. Hubby was watching the financial ticker the morning of 9/11. We saw the whole thing. I Watched for 2 weeks straight. Slept on the couch flipping between news channels waiting for them to say there were no planes and do some real investigative journalism. Only got up for bathroom breaks. After 2 weeks of no sleep and no hope of the truth coming out, and every person close to me being fully brainwashed in trauma based mind control, I unplugged the TV again. I was severely depressed for years. I went back to school a few months later for a music engineering degree. Music was the only thing that gave me any joy at the time. Clearly I am “one of the few” you refer to, though I doubt you will never acknowledge this about me. I “knew“ long before anyone else I have ever met. It’s been very lonely here, living in reality. I appreciate all the knowledge you have on history and politics. You are definitely “one of the few“ and even if you dont like me, at least I know I’m not alone anymore. Which is huge. And I also finally have hope that this “mystery illness” I’ve had since birth actually has a cure.

Let’s be CRYSTAL CLEAR: your centralized healthcare costs exploded, and your network of providers shrunk because of the implementation of Obamacare in 2008. This process began, however, in 1968 when healthcare was centralized by the LBJ.
Obamacare was the latest iteration of the 1968 law, and it now forces us to use failed ALGORITHMS such as the “community rating system.” Moreover, that’s why costs exploded afterward. If you believe in real math, not rehypothecation, then you know Obamacare will NEVER work. It is a fiat-based system based on theft of service. The system was based on overcoming and under-deliver care = to give you their version of "centralized health" using algorithms they designed to dictate care and what is the best evidence. The only algorithm you need is the cognitive bias nature provides, which is fully decentralized. This podcast gets deep into this.

If you cannot think well for your own health in this system, you are doomed to be a servant of those who use algorithms to do the thinking for you. The same idea is buried at the core of artificial intelligence networks. There are no real long-term benefits to the end user when you must understand how the algorithm was built and what it incentivizes.

Being decentralized in the modern world is not something to fear; it is something to conquer.

But that's what will make you different and unique--and over time, it will make you incredibly successful when you begin to write the next chapter of your life. It will be a book all will want to read when they see the words of your story spill all over the pages.

You laugh at me because I am different and decentralized. I laugh at you because you are all the same, fully centralized, and in slavery you cannot see, understand, or solve.

This podcast between two old MDs unfolds that story in front of your eyes.
